functions.name: CF
functions.description: Shorter version for FM.CF . Expects dictionary to be named CF with custom functions. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Parameters parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the function.

parameters.name: Parameters...
parameters.description: Value to pass as parameter.

examples.label: Call Concat function with two parameters:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CF"; "Concat"; "Hello"; "World")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.Compress
functions.description: Compresses a container value. Compression is not recommended for file formats which are normally compressed already. This includes PDF, various image formats like PNG and JPEG, video and audio files as well as zip archives. Compression works very well for text files. It is recommended to check with Container.GetTotalSize to see if compressed data is smaller. If you compress an already compressed container, the result may be bigger due to the archive header. Compressed containers can be detected by looking if the type list returned by Container.GetTypes contains an item named ZLIB. You can use Container.GetTypeSize to query the size of compressed data alone. Not recommended for bigger file sizes. Compression may take long enough that user doesn't like to wait. e.g. 20 seconds for 300 MB.

parameters.name: ContainerValue
parameters.description: The container value to compress.

examples.label: Compress container:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Container.Compress"; Container Compression::SomeContainer)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Container Compression" id="99999" name="Compressed"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.DecodeFromBase64
functions.description: Decodes data from base64 text into a container value. See also Container.GetBase64 . If you have a DynaPDF Pro license and DynaPDF functions initialized, this function can add previews for the PDF file on Windows and Linux. For MacOS, we make them with PDFKit.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type to return. Can be JPEG, TIFF, GIF, PNG, PDF or File.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: If type is container, this defines the file name to use.

parameters.name: DontRenderPreview
parameters.description: Pass 1 to not create a JPEG with preview of first page as part of the container. (only for returning PDF)

examples.label: From PDF in Base64 to a PNG in Base64:
examples.text_code:
# decode some PDF in base64 text
Set Variable [
$PDFData
; Value:
MBS
( "Container.DecodeFromBase64"; "PDF";
PDF_ToPNG::base64_PDF
; "test.pdf" )]
# render first page to a picture in PNG format
Set Variable [
$PNGData
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage
";
$PDFData
; 0; "PNG"; "test.png"; 150; 1; 0)]
# encode PNG as Base64
Set Variable [
$EncodedPNG
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.GetBase64
";
$PNGData
; "PNGf" )]
# store text in a field
Set Field [
PDF_ToPNG::Convert_text
;
$EncodedPNG
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> decode some PDF in base64 text</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.DecodeFromBase64"; "PDF"; PDF_ToPNG::base64_PDF; "test.pdf" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PDFData</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> render first page to a picture in PNG format</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage"; $PDFData; 0; "PNG"; "test.png"; 150; 1; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PNGData</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> encode PNG as Base64</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetBase64"; $PNGData; "PNGf" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$EncodedPNG</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> store text in a field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$EncodedPNG</Calculation>
    <Field table="PDF_ToPNG" id="99999" name="Convert_text"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.DecodeFromHex
functions.description: Decodes data from hex text into a container value. If you have a DynaPDF Pro license and DynaPDF functions initialized, this function can add previews for the PDF file on Windows and Linux. For MacOS, we make them with PDFKit.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type to return. Can be JPEG, TIFF, GIF, PNG, PDF or File.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to process.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: If filter is container, this defines the file name to use.

parameters.name: DontRenderPreview
parameters.description: Pass 1 to not create a JPEG with preview of first page as part of the container. (only for returning PDF)

examples.label: Decode hex encoded zlib compressed PDF in a text field:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
PDF::PDFFile
;
MBS
( "
Container.Decompress
";
MBS
( "
Container.RenameStream
";
MBS
( "Container.DecodeFromHex"; "FILE";
PDF::PDF_hex
; "test.pdf"); "FILE"; "ZLIB" )) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Container.Decompress"; MBS( "Container.RenameStream"; MBS( "Container.DecodeFromHex"; "FILE"; PDF::PDF_hex; "test.pdf"); "FILE"; "ZLIB" )) </Calculation>
    <Field table="PDF" id="99999" name="PDFFile "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.Decompress
functions.description: Decompresses a container value. If you pass a compressed container value, this function will decompress it and return the container value with uncompressed data. Compressed containers can be detected by looking if the type list returned by Container.GetTypes contains an item named ZLIB. You can use Container.GetTypeSize to query the size of compressed data alone. Does not work with FileMaker's compressed plugins in a container.

parameters.name: ContainerValue
parameters.description: The container value to decompress.

examples.label: Decompress container:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Container.Decompress"; Container Compression::Compressed)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Container Compression" id="99999" name="Decompressed"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Decode hex encoded zlib compressed PDF in a text field:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
PDF::PDFFile
;
MBS
( "Container.Decompress";
MBS
( "
Container.RenameStream
";
MBS
( "
Container.DecodeFromHex
"; "FILE";
PDF::PDF_hex
; "test.pdf"); "FILE"; "ZLIB" )) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Container.Decompress"; MBS( "Container.RenameStream"; MBS( "Container.DecodeFromHex"; "FILE"; PDF::PDF_hex; "test.pdf"); "FILE"; "ZLIB" )) </Calculation>
    <Field table="PDF" id="99999" name="PDFFile "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.Deserialize
functions.description: Restores a serialized container value.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The serialized data as text.

examples.label: Serialize to a text field and deseralize back:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Container.Serialize"; myTable::FirstContainerField) </Calculation>
    <Field table="myTable" id="99999" name="TextField "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Container.Deserialize"; myTable::TextField) </Calculation>
    <Field table="myTable" id="99999" name="OtherContainerField "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.Export
functions.signature: MBS( "Container.Export"; Container; Index or Type; FilePath )
functions.description: Writes selected data content from a container to a file path. Warning: Most people trying this function, really need the Container.WriteFile function! For example you can write out from a TIFF image stored in the database the TIFF data itself or the preview JPEG. Please note that types are always 4 characters long, "PDF " has a space on the end. Some of the allowed types include "TIFF", "PDF ", "PNGf", "GIFf", "BMPf", "EPS ", "PICT", "M4V ", "WAV ", "M4A ", "MOV ", "AVI ", "AU  ", "MP3 ", "MPEG", "MPG4", "AIFF", "8BPS", "FILE", "MAIN", "JPEG" and maybe some more. Use functions like Container.GetTypes or Container.GetType to check what is inside a container. To convert image formats, please review GraphicsMagick functions like GMImage.NewFromContainer and GMImage.WriteToPNGContainer . To convert PDF to PNG, check functions like PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage , DynaPDF.GeneratePreview or DynaPDF.RenderPage . This function is only for internal containers. Please use Container.WriteFile for all containers. You can use Files.ReadFile to read a file into a container field or variable. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container where the data is stored.

parameters.name: Index or Type
parameters.description: Specify what part of the container to export. e.g. "JPEG", "PDF ", "GIFf" or "PICT") or the index.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: Absolute path to store file.

functions.result: Returns "OK" on success or error message.

examples.label: Export JPEG file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.Export"; Files::data; "JPEG"; GetAsText(Files::data)+".jpg")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Export all streams in container to individual files:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$container
; Value:
Test::Image
]
#where to save
Set Variable [
$DesktopFolder
; Value:
MBS
("
Folders.UserDesktop
")]
#loop over streams in container
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.GetCount
";
$container
)]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:0]
Loop
#query the type of stream with given index
Set Variable [
$type
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.GetType
";
$container
;
$index
)]
#write it to a file
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
$DesktopFolder
& "/" &
$type
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Container.Export";
$container
;
$index
;
$path
)]
#next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$count
=
$index
]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Test::Image</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$container</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>where to save</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Folders.UserDesktop")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DesktopFolder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>loop over streams in container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetCount"; $container )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>query the type of stream with given index</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetType"; $container; $index )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$type</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>write it to a file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$DesktopFolder & "/" & $type</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.Export"; $container; $index; $path )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$count = $index</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Export a container to desktop:
examples.text_code:
# get path stored in the container field
Set Variable [
$ContainerPath
; Value: GetAsText (
Contacts::Photo
Container ) ]
# get the file name portion
Set Variable [
$ContainerName
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$ContainerPath
) ]
# take a folder on the computer, e.g. desktop
Set Variable [
$DesktopFolder
; Value:
MBS
( "
Folders.UserDesktop
" ) ]
# build path with file name on that folder
Set Variable [
$FilePath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$DesktopFolder
;
$ContainerName
) ]
# and export the content of container to that path
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.WriteFile
";
Contacts::Photo
Container;
$FilePath
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get path stored in the container field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsText ( Contacts::Photo Container ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ContainerPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get the file name portion</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; $ContainerPath ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ContainerName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> take a folder on the computer, e.g. desktop</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Folders.UserDesktop" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DesktopFolder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> build path with file name on that folder</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $DesktopFolder; $ContainerName ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$FilePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and export the content of container to that path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.WriteFile"; Contacts::Photo Container; $FilePath ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.ExtractStream

parameters.name: Container

parameters.name: InputType

parameters.name: NewName

parameters.name: OutputType
parameters.description: The output type.

functions.name: Container.GetBase64
functions.description: Returns the base 64 encoded value of the container. You must tell the plug-in which of the types in the container field you want to use. You can use either it's name (JPEG, TIFF, PNGf etc) or it's index. The index is from 0 to the count of types -1 You can use Container.GetCount to return the count of types in the container. You can pass index zero and get data form references file in case container is a file reference. See also Container.DecodeFromBase64 .

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: Name or Index
parameters.description: either the name of the Type or it's index. Version 12.2 returns main item if this parameter is empty.

examples.label: Get PNG image in Container as Base64:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.GetCount
functions.description: Counts the number of types in a container field. A container contains several data parts, each with an unique type. This returns the number of types in a container.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or variable containing container value.

examples.label: Export all streams in container to individual files:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$container
; Value:
Test::Image
]
#where to save
Set Variable [
$DesktopFolder
; Value:
MBS
("
Folders.UserDesktop
")]
#loop over streams in container
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "Container.GetCount";
$container
)]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:0]
Loop
#query the type of stream with given index
Set Variable [
$type
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.GetType
";
$container
;
$index
)]
#write it to a file
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
$DesktopFolder
& "/" &
$type
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.Export
";
$container
;
$index
;
$path
)]
#next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$count
=
$index
]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Test::Image</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$container</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DesktopFolder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>query the type of stream with given index</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetType"; $container; $index )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$type</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>write it to a file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$DesktopFolder & "/" & $type</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.Export"; $container; $index; $path )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$count = $index</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.GetDataURL
functions.description: Returns the complete Data url for an image stored in a container field. Performs base64 encoding and puts the data type in front of the output. This is very useful for displaying images in web pages. The function tries to make data urls from the images in the container field in the following order: Tiff, PNG, PDF, GIF, JPEG Unfortunately, FileMaker on Windows XP doesn't display image urls very well since it is based on Internet Explorer which doesn't like Image URLs. In plugin version this also works for referenced JPEG images. Updated in v12.3 to work fine for TIFF, PNG, BMP, GIF, JPEG and WebP.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

examples.label: Get URL for only PDF part and ignore JPEG/PNG previews:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.GetDataURL"; MBS( "Container.ExtractStream"; ContainerField; "PDF "; "test.pdf"; "PDF " ))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.GetHeight
functions.description: Returns the Height of the image in pixels. This is based on the metadata in the container (SIZE part) stored by FileMaker and not based on the actual content of the container.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

examples.label: Get the Height
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetWidth" ; ContainerField )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.GetHex
functions.description: Returns the hex encoded value of the container. You must tell the plug-in which of the types in the container field you want to use. You can use either it's name (JPEG, TIFF, PNGf etc) or it's index. The index is from 0 to the count of types -1 You can use Container.GetCount to return the count of types in the container. You can pass index zero and get data form references file in case container is a file reference.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: Name or Index
parameters.description: Either the name of the Type or it's index Version 12.2 returns main item if this parameter is empty.

examples.label: Get Container as Hex:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetHex"; MyTable::MyContainerField )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.GetMimeType
functions.description: Returns the mime type for an image stored in a container field. This is very useful for displaying images in web pages. Used internally in Container.GetDataURL function. When this returns "application/octet-stream", you could get the file name and use Text.FileExtensionToMimeType .

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

examples.label: Query mime type for photo container:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetMimeType"; Contacts::Photo )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.GetName
functions.description: Queries the path list from a container. This is not the same as reading container as text. The result may be a file path, several file paths, a file name or nothing.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

functions.result: Returns the path list or error.

functions.name: Container.GetResolution
functions.description: Queries the DPI settings stored in a container. This does not look into the image resolution of an image, but just in the metadata FileMaker stored there when importing the image. FileMaker stores resolution in "DPI " part in the container. If selector x or y are passed, we return just the DPI value in x or y direction. If no selector is passed, we return list with both values. First entry is x, second y.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Optional, if you pass "x" or "y" only the requested value is returned.

examples.label: Query resolution:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.GetSize
functions.description: Returns the size of the container. A container contains several data parts with data and this function queries size in bytes of one of them. You must pass either the name of the type you wish to get or its index. Index start at 0. See Container.GetTotalSize to query size of all data in a container.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: Name or index

examples.label: Query size of container with PNG:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.GetSize"; Images::Image; "PNGf")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.GetText
functions.signature: MBS( "Container.GetText"; Container; Name or Index { ; Encoding } )
functions.description: Returns the text value of the container data. You must tell the plug-in which of the types in the container field you want to use. You can use either it's name (JPEG, TIFF, PNGf etc) or it's index. The index is from 0 to the count of types -1 You can use Container.GetCount to return the count of types in the container. You can pass index zero and get data form references file in case container is a file reference. See also Text.ReadTextFromContainer for reading text from main item in container.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: Name or Index
parameters.description: either the name of the Type or it's index

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Show main item in container:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Main item"; MBS("Container.GetText"; test::Test</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"MAIN")</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query text of XML file in a container:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetText"; test::test; "FILE"; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.GetTotalSize
functions.description: Returns the size of the whole container in bytes. A file is typically broken up into several object types when stored in a container field. This function returns the total size of all those objects.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

examples.label: Get Total Size
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.GetType
functions.description: Returns the type at the given index (zero based). A container contains several data parts, each with an unique type. Types are 4 character long and some types like "PDF " have an extra space. See Container.GetTypes to query list of all types in a container.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the the item to query in the container.

functions.name: Container.GetTypeSize
functions.description: Returns the size of the indicated object type in container field. For referenced files, this function will just return the size of the referenced file independent of the type. See Container.GetTotalSize to query size of all data in a container.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type code for the type to check. e.g. "JPEG" for a jpeg image.

examples.label: Get size of PNG image
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetTypeSize" ; ContainerField; "PNGf" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: Container.GetTypes
functions.description: Returns the list of objects in the container field. FileMaker stores a copy of the image in the container field as a JPEG if the image is not already of that type. That is why you will always see JPEG in the list of Types, even if the image in question is a PNG. This function will have "FILE" in the list of types if the image has File inserted into it. Some of the allowed types include "TIFF", "PDF ", "PNGf", "GIFf", "BMPf", "EPS ", "PICT", "FILE" and "JPEG". "ZLIB" is the type used by compressed containers. This function does not work for unencrypted external containers.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

examples.label: Get Types
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetTypes" ; ContainerField )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query PDF URL if there is PDF, otherwise use picture for data URL:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
// take it from source container field
Container = ContainerField;
// now check what's inside
types = MBS( "Container.GetTypes"; Container );
// has PDF content?
Container = If(Position(types; "PDF "; 1; 1) > 0;
// extract PDF part
   MBS( "Container.ExtractStream"; ContainerField; "PDF "; "test.pdf"; "PDF " ); 
// otherwise use container as is
   Container);
// now make URL
result = MBS("Container.GetDataURL"; Container)
];result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.description: Returns the Width of the image in pixels. This is based on the metadata in the container (SIZE part) stored by FileMaker and not based on the actual content of the container.

functions.name: Container.IsCompressed
functions.description: Queries whether container is compressed. Returns 1 if compressed and 0 if not.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

functions.name: Container.ReadArchive
functions.description: Reads an archived container. Please use Container.WriteArchive to create the archive. The zip file must have a special format to be read successful, so probably only archives created by Container.WriteArchive will work. This works for any content of a container, except for referenced container files. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the archive file to use.

examples.label: Reads an archive:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.ReadArchive"; "C:\Benutzer\Christian\Desktop\test.txt")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Writes an archive:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.WriteArchive"; MyTable::MyContainer; "C:\Benutzer\Christian\Desktop\test.txt")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.ReadFile
functions.description: Reads a file with the given path. Reads the data in the file and returns as container. See also Text.ReadTextFile for reading text files with specific encoding. For reading simple text files, please use Text.ReadTextFile . See also Files.ReadFile , Files.ReadJPEG , Files.ReadPDF , Files.ReadPNG , Text.ReadTextFile and RichText.ReadFile . This function is optimized for FM 14 and newer to stream file content when using auto mode. This allows to import up to 4 GB of data in small chunks. FileMaker may decide to create a preview when the plugin passes back the data to FileMaker. If FileMaker on macOS creates a preview for a PDF may take some time and leak memory. This function reads the file data into memory and does not define how FileMaker stores it. This is defined by which field it is later assigned to. And the field options define whether this container value is stored internally or externally. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path. Something like "/Users/cs/desktop/test.txt" on Mac and "C:\Programs\Data\test.txt" on Windows. Files without path end in the root directory on Mac.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: If missing or "auto", the plugin will try to detect type by file name extension. If filter is "container", the result is returned as a container with a FILE stream inside. The type can be specific here like PDF, JPEG, TIFF, GIF, BMP or PSD to return as image container. Or "compressed" to return a compressed container.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: If result is container, this defines the file name to use.

examples.label: Import all files in a folder:
examples.text_code:
# list files in a folder
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Files.List
";
$FolderPath
) ]
# loop over the files
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: ValueCount (
$r
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
If [
$count
>0 ]
Loop
# build path
Set Variable [
$fileName
; GetValue (
$r
;
$index
+1  )]
Set Variable [
$filePath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$FolderPath
;
$fileName
)]
Set Variable [
$fileData
; Value:
MBS
("Container.ReadFile";
$filePath
)]
# create record for new file
New Record/Request
Set Field [
MyData::Container
;
$fileData
]
Set Field [
MyData::Name
;
$fileName
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
=
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.List"; $FolderPath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over the files</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount ( $r ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count>0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> build path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue ( $r ; $index+1  )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fileName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $FolderPath; $fileName )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.ReadFile"; $filePath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fileData</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> create record for new file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$fileData </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyData" id="99999" name="Container"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$fileName </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyData" id="99999" name="Name"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index = $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.ReadImage
functions.description: Reads and convert image. This function is useful to read all the image formats supported by Apple's frameworks and convert them to a common format like JPEG or PNG. Use this function to convert old PICT images in containers. Works as well for HEIC and HEIF images. Added Windows support in v13.0. You need to install HEIF Image Extensions from the Windows App Store. Reads webp files on macOS. Supports JPEG-XL files for macOS 15.2 and iOS 18.2 or newer. Please use Container.ReadFile on Linux instead.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

Contacts::Photo

    <Restore state="False"/>

  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Read Image Failed?" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$image </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Read image and scale down as thumbnail:
examples.text_code:
# Try plugin to read more formats known by OS
Set Variable [
$imageData
; Value:
MBS
( "Container.ReadImage";
$input
; "PNG"; "image.png") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 and not IsEmpty (
$imageData
) ]
# scale down?
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewFromContainer
";
$imageData
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
# limit size to 1000 pixel maximum
Set Variable [
$scale
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Scale
";
$Image
; "1000>") ]
# remove metadata
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetProfile
";
$image
; "IPTC"; "" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetProfile
";
$image
; "EXIF"; "" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.SetProfile
";
$image
; "XMP"; "" ) ]
# save
Set Variable [
$NewImage
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer
";
$Image
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Preview
;
$NewImage
]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Made
using ; "ReadImage" ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
GMImage.Release
";
$Image
) ]
End If
#
Set Field [
Get Preview::Preview
;
$imageData
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Try plugin to read more formats known by OS</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.ReadImage"; $input; "PNG"; "image.png") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$imageData</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 and not IsEmpty ( $imageData ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> scale down?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewFromContainer"; $imageData) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> limit size to 1000 pixel maximum</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.Scale";$Image; "1000>") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$scale</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> remove metadata</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetProfile"; $image; "IPTC"; "" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetProfile"; $image; "EXIF"; "" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.SetProfile"; $image; "XMP"; "" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> save</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMImage.WriteToJPEGContainer"; $Image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NewImage</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$NewImage </Calculation>
    <Field table="Get Preview" id="99999" name="Preview "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"ReadImage" </Calculation>
    <Field table="Get Preview" id="99999" name="Made using "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("GMimage.Release";$Image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$imageData </Calculation>
    <Field table="Get Preview" id="99999" name="Preview "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.ReadImageFile
functions.description: Reads and convert image file. This function is useful to read all the image formats supported by Apple's frameworks and convert them to a common format like JPEG or PNG. Use this function to convert old PICT or new HEIC and HEIF images. Added Windows support in v13.0. You need to install HEIF Image Extensions from the Windows App Store. Reads webp files on macOS. Supports JPEG-XL files for macOS 15.2 and iOS 18.2 or newer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

examples.label: Read in some picture and convert to PNG:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.ReadImageFile"; "/Users/cs/Pictures/mond.jpg"; "PNG") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.Remove
functions.description: Removes a stream from container value. This function can be used to remove the JPEG preview from an image. Or if you have a container with PDF and preview, you could remove the PDF to just get the preview image. Use Container.GetTypes to learn what types are inside a container.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type code of the stream to remove. Can be "PDF ", "JPEG" or one of the other usual types.

functions.result: Returns modified container value or error.

examples.label: Renames an image to have a new file extension.
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.Remove"; $PDFContainer; "JPEG" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$jpegOnly</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Remove PDF from container value:

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.Remove"; FinalPDF; "PDF " )
/* PDF produced with DynaPDF Pro will have Preview image. Removing PDF will reveal the preview: */</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: Container.RemovePreview
functions.description: Removes the preview from a container value. Check with Container.GetTypes what a container contains. If a PDF contains a JPEG or PNG for preview, this function removes it. Also removes JPEG preview for PNG or TIFF images. If you remove preview and assign the value to a field, FileMaker may decide to recreate a preview later.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container where the data is stored.

examples.label: Remove preview:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
MyTable::Output
; Value:
MBS
("Container.RemovePreview";
MyTable::Input
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Value: MBS("Container.RemovePreview"; MyTable::Input) </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyTable" id="99999" name="Output"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.Rename
functions.description: Renames the file path in a container value. Does only work for normal containers, not with referenced files. This is more efficient than combining Base64Encode/Decode since our plugin just changes the name metadata and leaves the data in place. For external containers, FileMaker renames the file when the plugin renames the metadata. Or it writes a new file and later deletes old file. Later may be when FileMaker quits.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: New file name or new path list.

functions.result: Returns modified container value or error.

examples.label: Renames an image to have a new file extension.
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$oldname
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.GetName
";
$container
) ]
Set Variable [
$newname
; Value: substitute(
$oldname
; ".jpg"; ".png") ]
Set Variable [
$newcontainer
; Value:
MBS
( "Container.Rename";
$container
;
$newname
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.GetName"; $container ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$oldname</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>substitute($oldname; ".jpg"; ".png") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$newname</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.Rename"; $container; $newname ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$newcontainer</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

test::file

]

examples.xml_code:

examples.label: Rename a container field:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
Anlagen::Bild
;
MBS
( "Container.Rename";
Anlagen::Bild
; "new.jpg" ) ]
# with secure storage it was "remote:old.jpg" before and after rename it is "remote:new.jpg"
# with open storage it also writes new file and later FileMaker deletes the file when FMP closes.
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">

    <Field table="Anlagen" id="99999" name="Bild "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> with secure storage it was "remote:old.jpg" before and after rename it is "remote:new.jpg"</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> with open storage it also writes new file and later FileMaker deletes the file when FMP closes.</Text>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.RenameStream
functions.description: Renames a stream in a container. See Container.GetTypes to figure out what stream types a container value has.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to inspect.

parameters.name: InputType
parameters.description: The input type.

parameters.name: OutputType
parameters.description: The output type.

examples.label: Rename JPEG like a file import:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.RenameStream"; Test::InputContainer; "JPEG"; "FILE")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Decode hex encoded zlib compressed PDF in a text field:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
PDF::PDFFile
;
MBS
( "
Container.Decompress
";
MBS
( "Container.RenameStream";
MBS
( "
Container.DecodeFromHex
"; "FILE";
PDF::PDF_hex
; "test.pdf"); "FILE"; "ZLIB" )) ]
examples.xml_code:

functions.name: Container.Screenshot
functions.description: Takes screenshot from a named control in FileMaker. This function was developed to take a screenshot of a container control, but it also works for any other control like WebViewers.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Name of the control on screen.

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.Serialize
functions.description: Serialized a container as a text. The container content is stored in a base64 encoded string which the plugin function Container.Deserialize can read. Does not work with external containers. Using this function allows to store a container in a text field or transmit it with Socket functions, SerialPort functions or by writing it to a file.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to serialize.

examples.label: Serialize to a text field and deseralize back:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Container.Serialize"; myTable::FirstContainerField) </Calculation>
    <Field table="myTable" id="99999" name="TextField "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Container.Deserialize"; myTable::TextField) </Calculation>
    <Field table="myTable" id="99999" name="OtherContainerField "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.WriteArchive
functions.description: Writes an archive with container value. This function allows you to archive whatever the container contains into a zip archive. Please use Container.ReadArchive to read back. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container field or value to archive.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native path to the archive file to use.

examples.label: Reads an archive:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.ReadArchive"; "C:\Benutzer\Christian\Desktop\test.txt")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Writes an archive:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.WriteArchive"; MyTable::MyContainer; "C:\Benutzer\Christian\Desktop\test.txt")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Container.WriteFile
functions.description: Writes a file with the given container. See also Files.WriteFile for more options. See FileDialog functions if you like to show a save dialog to pick a destination for the file path. You can use Files.ReadFile to read a file into a container field or variable. If you need to create directory first, please use Files.CreateDirectory function. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The container field or value to save.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path. Something like "/Users/cs/desktop/test.jpg" on Mac and "C:\Programs\Data\test.jpg" on Windows. Files without path end in the root directory on Mac.

examples.label: Read a file and write it elsewhere

$Image
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.ReadFile
"; "/Users/cs/Pictures/Test.jpg")]

$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Container.WriteFile";
$Image
; "/Users/cs/Desktop/target.jpg")]
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.WriteFile"; $Image; "/Users/cs/Desktop/target.jpg")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Export a container to desktop:
examples.text_code:
# get path stored in the container field
Set Variable [
$ContainerPath
; Value: GetAsText (
Contacts::Photo
Container ) ]
# get the file name portion
Set Variable [
$ContainerName
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$ContainerPath
) ]
# take a folder on the computer, e.g. desktop
Set Variable [
$DesktopFolder
; Value:
MBS
( "
Folders.UserDesktop
" ) ]
# build path with file name on that folder
Set Variable [
$FilePath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$DesktopFolder
;
$ContainerName
) ]
# and export the content of container to that path
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Container.WriteFile";
Contacts::Photo
Container;
$FilePath
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get path stored in the container field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsText ( Contacts::Photo Container ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ContainerPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get the file name portion</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; $ContainerPath ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ContainerName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> take a folder on the computer, e.g. desktop</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Folders.UserDesktop" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DesktopFolder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> build path with file name on that folder</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $DesktopFolder; $ContainerName ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$FilePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and export the content of container to that path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.WriteFile"; Contacts::Photo Container; $FilePath ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Write a HTML file and an image file to load in web viewer:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [ $folder ; Value: MBS ( " Folders.UserTemporary " ) ] Set Variable [ $path ; Value: MBS ( " Path.AddPathComponent "; $folder ; "test.jpg" ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( "Container.WriteFile"; Images::Image ; $path ) ] # Set Variable [ $html ; Value: "

Hello World

" ] Set Variable [ $path ; Value: MBS ( " Path.AddPathComponent "; $folder ; "test.html" ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " Text.WriteTextFile "; $html ; $path ) ] # Set Variable [ $URL ; Value: MBS ( " Path.FilePathToFileURL "; $Path ) ] Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: $URL ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Folders.UserTemporary" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$folder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $folder; "test.jpg" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.WriteFile"; Images::Image; $path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"<html><body><p>Hello World</p><img src=test.jpg width=300></body></html>" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$html</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $folder; "test.html" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.WriteTextFile"; $html; $path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.FilePathToFileURL"; $Path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$URL</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="146" name="Set Web Viewer">
    <Action value="GoToURL"/>
    <ObjectName>
      <Calculation>"web"</Calculation>
    </ObjectName>
    <URL custom="False">
      <Calculation>$URL </Calculation>
    </URL>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CoreLocation.ClearFailHandler
functions.description: Clears the script called for errors.

functions.name: CoreLocation.ClearUpdateLocationHandler
functions.description: Clears the script called for new location.

functions.name: CoreLocation.RegionMonitoringAvailable
functions.description: Whether region monitoring is supported on the current device. Support for region monitoring may not be available on all devices and models. You should check the value of this property before attempting to set up any regions or initiate region monitoring. Even if region monitoring support is present on a device, it may still be unavailable because the user disabled it for the current app or for all apps.

functions.name: CoreLocation.RegionMonitoringEnabled
functions.description: Whether region monitoring is currently enabled. In iOS, the user can enable or disable location services (including region monitoring) using the controls in Settings > Location Services. You should check the return value of this method before starting region monitoring updates to determine whether the user currently allows location services to be used at all. If this method returns false and you start region monitoring updates anyway, the Core Location framework prompts the user to confirm asking whether location services should be reenabled. This method does not check to see if region monitoring capabilities are actually supported by the device. Therefore, you should also check the return value of the regionMonitoringAvailable() class method before attempting to start region monitoring services.

functions.name: CoreLocation.SetFailEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression evaluated when CoreLocation wants to report an error.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: CoreLocation.SetFailHandler
functions.description: Sets the script called when CoreLocation wants to report an error. Scriptparameter is a localized error message. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker file that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers drag action handler:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CoreLocation.SetFailHandler"; Get ( FileName ); "CoreLocationError")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression evaluated when a new location has been detected. You can query current location with the other location functions. You don't need to install this evaluation to read location. You can always simply call location functions to get last known location.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler
functions.description: Sets the script called when a new location has been detected. You can query current location with the other location functions. You don't need to install this script call to read location. You can always simply call location functions to get last known location. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers drag action handler:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler"; Get ( FileName ); "CoreLocationUpdate")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CoreLocation.altitude
functions.description: The altitude measured in meters. Positive values indicate altitudes above sea level. Negative values indicate altitudes below sea level. This function queries current know location. It's updated from time to time. To receive a script call when new location is available, please use CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler . Returns zero if not location is available. See CoreLocation.hasLocation function.

examples.label: Query altitude:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CoreLocation.altitude") </Calculation>
    <Field table="CoreLocation" id="99999" name="altitude "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CoreLocation.authorizationStatus
functions.description: Returns the app’s authorization status for using location services. The authorization status of a given app is managed by the system and determined by several factors. Apps must be explicitly authorized to use location services by the user and location services must themselves currently be enabled for the system. A request for user authorization is displayed automatically when your app first attempts to use location services.

parameters.name: restricted
parameters.description: This app is not authorized to use location services.

parameters.name: denied
parameters.description: The user explicitly denied the use of location services for this app or location services are currently disabled in Settings.

parameters.name: authorizedAlways
parameters.description: This app is authorized to start location services at any time.

parameters.name: authorizedWhenInUse
parameters.description: This app is authorized to start most location services while running in the foreground.

functions.name: CoreLocation.course
functions.description: Returns the direction in which the device is traveling. Course values are measured in degrees starting at due north and continuing clockwise around the compass. Thus, north is 0 degrees, east is 90 degrees, south is 180 degrees, and so on. Course values may not be available on all devices. A negative value indicates that the direction is invalid. This function queries current know location. It's updated from time to time. To receive a script call when new location is available, please use CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler .

examples.label: Query course:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CoreLocation.course") </Calculation>
    <Field table="CoreLocation" id="99999" name="course "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CoreLocation.description
functions.description: Returns the location data in a formatted text string. A string of the form "<<latitude>, <longitude>> +/- <accuracy>m (speed <speed> kph / heading <heading>) @ <date-time>", where <latitude>, <longitude>, <accuracy>, <speed>, and <heading> are formatted floating point numbers and <date-time> is a formatted date string that includes date, time, and time zone information. The returned string is intended for display purposes only. This function queries current know location. It's updated from time to time. To receive a script call when new location is available, please use CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler .

examples.label: Query description:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CoreLocation.description") </Calculation>
    <Field table="CoreLocation" id="99999" name="description "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CoreLocation.desiredAccuracy
functions.description: The desired accuracy of the location data.

functions.name: CoreLocation.dismissHeadingCalibrationDisplay
functions.description: Dismisses the heading calibration view from the screen immediately. Core Location uses the heading calibration alert to calibrate the available heading hardware as needed. The display of this view is automatic, assuming your delegate supports displaying the view at all. If the view is displayed, you can use this method to dismiss it after an appropriate amount of time to ensure that your app’s user interface is not unduly disrupted. Added macOS support with MBS Plugin 11.1 for macOS 10.15 and newer.

functions.name: CoreLocation.distanceFilter
functions.description: Queries distance filter setting. The minimum distance (measured in meters) a device must move laterally before an update event is generated.

functions.name: CoreLocation.distanceFromLocation
functions.description: Returns the distance (in meters) from the receiver's location to the specified location. This method measures the distance between the two locations by tracing a line between them that follows the curvature of the Earth. The resulting arc is a smooth curve and does not take into account specific altitude changes between the two locations.

parameters.name: Latitude
parameters.description: The latitude value for the geo coordinate.

parameters.name: Longitude
parameters.description: The latitude value for the geo coordinate.

examples.label: Return distance from London
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CoreLocation.distanceFromLocation"; 51.50939, -0.11832)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CoreLocation.hasLocation
functions.description: Queries whether we have a location. You may need to call CoreLocation.startUpdatingLocation to start getting locations.

functions.name: CoreLocation.headingAvailable
functions.description: Whether heading is available. Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the location manager is able to generate heading-related events. Heading data may not be available on Macs.
functions.result: Returns 1 if heading data is available; 0 if it is not.

functions.name: CoreLocation.horizontalAccuracy
functions.description: The radius of uncertainty for the location, measured in meters. The location's latitude and longitude identify the center of the circle, and this value indicates the radius of that circle. A negative value indicates that the location's latitude and longitude are invalid. This function queries current know location. It's updated from time to time. To receive a script call when new location is available, please use CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler .

CoreLocation::horizontalAccuracy
;
MBS
("CoreLocation.horizontalAccuracy") ]

CoreLocation::verticalAccuracy
;

("
CoreLocation.verticalAccuracy
") ]

functions.description: Returns the geographical coordinate information. This function queries current know location. It's updated from time to time. To receive a script call when new location is available, please use CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler . Returns zero if not location is available. See CoreLocation.hasLocation function.
