functions.name: CoreLocation.locationServicesEnabled
functions.description: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether location services are enabled on the device. The user can enable or disable location services altogether from the Settings application by toggling the switch in Settings > General > Location Services. You should check the return value of this method before starting location updates to determine if the user has location services enabled for the current device. If this method returns 0 and you start location updates anyway, the Core Location framework prompts the user with a confirmation panel asking whether location services should be reenabled.
functions.result: Returns 1 if location services are enabled or 0 if they are not.

functions.name: CoreLocation.longitude
functions.description: Returns the geographical coordinate information. This function queries current know location. It's updated from time to time. To receive a script call when new location is available, please use CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler . Returns zero if not location is available. See CoreLocation.hasLocation function.

examples.label: Query position:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
CoreLocation::latitude
;
MBS
("
CoreLocation.latitude
") ]
Set Field [
CoreLocation::longitude
;
MBS
("CoreLocation.longitude") ]
examples.xml_code:

functions.name: CoreLocation.purpose
functions.description: An application-provided string that describes the reason for using location services. If the value of this property is non-nil, the system includes the string in the dialog that asks the user whether it is all right for your application to use location services. The string gives you a chance to explain the reason for why your application is using location services. You must set the value of this property prior to calling the CoreLocation.startUpdatingLocation method. Because the string is ultimately displayed to the user, you should always load it from a localized strings file. Available in macOS 10.7 and later.

functions.name: CoreLocation.requestAlwaysAuthorization
functions.description: Requests permission to use location services whenever the app is running. When the current authorization status is notDetermined, this method runs asynchronously and prompts the user to grant permission to the app to use location services. The user prompt contains the text from the NSLocationAlwaysUsageDescription key in your app’s Info.plist file, and the presence of that key is required when calling this method. See also https://developer.apple.com/documentation/corelocation/cllocationmanager/1620551-requestalwaysauthorization

functions.name: CoreLocation.requestLocation
functions.description: Requests the one-time delivery of the user’s current location. This method returns immediately. Calling it causes the location manager to obtain a location fix (which may take several seconds). Added macOS support with MBS Plugin 11.1 for macOS 10.14 and newer.

functions.name: CoreLocation.requestTemporaryFullAccuracyAuthorization
functions.description: Asks for temporary access. If your app doesn't have permission to access accurate location (see accuracyAuthorization), you can use this method to request temporary access to accurate location.  This access will expire automatically, but it won't expire while the user is still engaged with your app.  So, for example, while your app is in the foreground your app will retain the temporary access it was granted. Similarly, if your app starts a Continuous Background Location session with the background location indicator enabled (see -showsBackgroundLocationIndicator), your access to accurate location will remain as long as the background location indicator remains enabled.  This allows your app to provide session-oriented experiences which require accurate location (e.g. fitness or navigation), even if the user has decided not to grant your app persistent access to accurate location. When CoreLocation prepares the prompt for display, it will look at the NSLocationTemporaryUsageDescriptionDictionary key in your Info.plist.  The value should be a dictionary containing usage descriptions.  The purposeKey you provide to this method must correspond to an entry in that dictionary.  To retrieve a localized string, CoreLocation will load your InfoPlist.strings file and try to look up a string using the purposeKey you provided.  If that fails, CoreLocation will use the content provided in your Info.plist.  For example, suppose your Info.plist contains the following. <key>NSLocationTemporaryUsageDescriptionDictionary</key> <dict> <key>ExampleUsageDescription</key> <string>This app needs accurate location so it can verify that you're in a supported region.</string> <key>AnotherUsageDescription</key> <string>This app needs accurate location so it can show you relevant results.</string> </dict> In this case, you would be able to provide either "ExampleUsageDescription" or "AnotherUsageDescription" for the purposeKey argument.  Passing any other string would result in the prompt not being displayed.  To localize these usage descriptions, you would add entries for "ExampleUsageDescription" and "AnotherUsageDescription" to your InfoPlist.strings file. CoreLocation may decide against showing the user a prompt.  For example, if your app already has access to accurate location or it is currently in the background. Due to the info plist changes, this may only work in iOS apps. For iOS 14.0 and macOS 11.0 or newer.

parameters.name: purpose
parameters.description: The purpose text.

functions.name: CoreLocation.requestWhenInUseAuthorization
functions.description: Requests permission to use location services while the app is in the foreground. When the current authorization status is notDetermined, this method runs asynchronously and prompts the user to grant permission to the app to use location services. The user prompt contains the text from the NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription key in your app’s Info.plist file, and the presence of that key is required when calling this method. Added macOS support with MBS Plugin 11.1 for macOS 10.15 and newer.

functions.name: CoreLocation.setDesiredAccuracy
functions.description: Sets the desired accuracy of the location data. The receiver does its best to achieve the requested accuracy; however, the actual accuracy is not guaranteed. You should assign a value to this property that is appropriate for your usage scenario. In other words, if you need only the current location within a few kilometers, you should not specify "best" for the accuracy. Determining a location with greater accuracy requires more time and more power. When requesting high accuracy location data, the initial event delivered by the location service may not have the accuracy you requested. The location service delivers the initial event as quickly as possible. It then continues to determine the location with the accuracy you requested and delivers additional events, as necessary, when that data is available.

parameters.name: Accuracy
parameters.description: The new value. Can be a number or the folling text strings: Best, NearestTenMeters, HundredMeters, Kilometer and ThreeKilometers. Default is "best".

functions.name: CoreLocation.setDistanceFilter
functions.description: Sets distance filter. Sets the minimum distance (measured in meters) a device must move laterally before an update event is generated. This distance is measured relative to the previously delivered location. Use the value "none" to be notified of all movements. The default value of this property is "none".

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The distance. Either a number of text "none".

functions.name: CoreLocation.setPurpose
functions.description: Sets the application-provided string that describes the reason for using location services. If the value of this property is non-nil, the system includes the string in the dialog that asks the user whether it is all right for your application to use location services. The string gives you a chance to explain the reason for why your application is using location services. You must set the value of this property prior to calling the CoreLocation.startUpdatingLocation method. Because the string is ultimately displayed to the user, you should always load it from a localized strings file. Available in macOS 10.7 and later.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: Your purpose message.

functions.name: CoreLocation.significantLocationChangeMonitoringAvailable
functions.description: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether significant location change tracking is available. This method indicates whether the device is able to report updates based on significant location changes only. (Significant location change monitoring primarily involves detecting changes in the cell tower currently associated with the device.) This capability provides tremendous power savings for applications that want to track a user's approximate location and do not need highly accurate position information.
functions.result: Returns 1 if location change monitoring is available; 0 if it is not.

functions.name: CoreLocation.speed
functions.description: The instantaneous speed of the device in meters per second. This value reflects the instantaneous speed of the device in the direction of its current heading. A negative value indicates an invalid speed. Because the actual speed can change many times between the delivery of subsequent location events, you should use this property for informational purposes only. This function queries current know location. It's updated from time to time. To receive a script call when new location is available, please use CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler .

examples.label: Query speed:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CoreLocation.speed") </Calculation>
    <Field table="CoreLocation" id="99999" name="speed "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CoreLocation.startMonitoringSignificantLocationChanges
functions.description: Starts the generation of updates based on significant location changes. This method initiates the delivery of location events asynchronously, returning shortly after you call it. Location events are delivered to your didUpdateLocation script. The first event to be delivered is usually the most recently cached location event (if any) but may be a newer event in some circumstances. Obtaining a current location fix may take several additional seconds, so be sure to check the timestamps on the location events in your delegate method. Your fail script may be called if an error occurs.

functions.name: CoreLocation.startUpdatingHeading
functions.description: Starts the generation of updates that report the user’s current heading. This method returns immediately. Calling this method when the receiver is stopped causes it to obtain an initial heading and notify your delegate. After that, the receiver generates update events when the value in the headingFilter property is exceeded. Before calling this method, you should always check the headingAvailable property to see whether heading information is supported on the current device. If heading information is not supported, calling this method has no effect and does not result in the delivery of events to your delegate. Calling this method several times in succession does not automatically result in new events being generated. Calling stopUpdatingHeading() in between, however, does cause a new initial event to be sent the next time you call this method. If you start this service and your app is suspended, the system stops the delivery of events until your app starts running again (either in the foreground or background). If your app is terminated, the delivery of new heading events stops altogether and must be restarted by your code when the app is relaunched. Added macOS support with MBS Plugin 11.1 for macOS 10.15 and newer.

functions.name: CoreLocation.startUpdatingLocation
functions.description: Starts the generation of updates that report the user's current location. This method returns immediately. Calling this method when the receiver is stopped causes it to obtain an initial location fix (which may take several seconds) and notify your script. After that, the receiver generates update events primarily when the value in the distanceFilter property is exceeded. Updates may be delivered in other situations though. For example, the receiver may send another notification if the hardware gathers a more accurate location reading. Calling this method several times in succession does not automatically result in new events being generated. Calling stopUpdatingLocation in between, however, does cause a new initial event to be sent the next time you call this method. Available in macOS 10.6 and later.

functions.name: CoreLocation.stopMonitoringSignificantLocationChanges
functions.description: Stops the delivery of location events based on significant location changes. Use this method to stop the delivery of location events that was started using the CoreLocation.startMonitoringSignificantLocationChanges method.

functions.name: CoreLocation.stopUpdatingHeading
functions.description: Stops the generation of heading updates. Call this method whenever your code no longer needs to receive heading-related events. Disabling event delivery gives the receiver the option of disabling the appropriate hardware (and thereby saving power) when no clients need location data. You can always restart the generation of heading updates by calling the startUpdatingHeading() method again.

functions.name: CoreLocation.stopUpdatingLocation
functions.description: Stops the generation of location updates. You should call this method whenever your code no longer needs to receive location-related events. Disabling event delivery gives the receiver the option of disabling the appropriate hardware (and thereby saving power) when no clients need location data. You can always restart the generation of location updates by calling the CoreLocation.startUpdatingLocation method again.

functions.name: CoreLocation.timestamp
functions.description: Returns the time at which this location was determined. This function queries current know location. It's updated from time to time. To receive a script call when new location is available, please use CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler .

examples.label: Query timestamp:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("CoreLocation.timestamp") </Calculation>
    <Field table="CoreLocation" id="99999" name="timestamp "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CoreLocation.verticalAccuracy
functions.description: Returns the accuracy of the altitude value in meters. The value in the altitude property could be plus or minus the value indicated by this property. A negative value indicates that the altitude value is invalid. Determining the vertical accuracy requires a device with GPS capabilities. This function queries current know location. It's updated from time to time. To receive a script call when new location is available, please use CoreLocation.SetUpdateLocationHandler .

;
MBS
("

MBS
("CoreLocation.verticalAccuracy") ]

functions.name: Dialog.AddField
functions.signature: MBS( "Dialog.AddField"; Label { ; Text; Placeholder; Password; TextViewHeight } )
functions.description: Adds a field for the dialog. Please use Dialog.GetFieldText later to query text for field. Returns the index of the field. Maximum we allow 10 fields.

parameters.name: Label
parameters.description: The label text for the field.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text for the field. This is initial text.

parameters.name: Placeholder
parameters.description: The placeholder text shown when no text is in the field. (not for text view)

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: Pass 1 to hide text with • or 0 for normal text field.

parameters.name: TextViewHeight
parameters.description: Pass number of points in height for a textView. If value is >20, we will use a text view instead of a text fiel.d

examples.label: Adds two fields:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Dialog.AddField"; "Username:"; ""; "username here")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Dialog.AddField"; "Password:"; ""; "password here"; 1)]
examples.xml_code:

      <Calculation>MBS("Dialog.AddField"; "Username:"; ""; "username here")</Calculation>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Dialog.AddField"; "Password:"; ""; "password here"; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dialog.ClearFields
functions.description: Clears all fields.

functions.name: Dialog.CloseSheet
functions.description: Closes a sheet if there is one running. This will not call the script for the sheet notification. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 26th March 2016 Dialog.ClearFields - Dialog.GetAlternateButton 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: Dialog.GetAlternateButton
functions.description: Queries the title for the alternate button.

functions.name: Dialog.GetButton
functions.description: Queries button text.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the button. From 0 to 9.

functions.name: Dialog.GetButtonPressed
functions.description: Queries index of selected button. Value is negative If not applicable. Index is 0 for first button.

[
_ =
MBS
("
Dialog.Reset
") ;
_ =
MBS
("
Dialog.SetDefaultButton
" ; "Yes") ;
_ =
MBS
("
Dialog.SetOtherButton
" ; "No") ;
_ =
MBS

Dialog.SetAlternateButton

MBS

" ; "Delete the old file?") ;

MBS
("
Dialog.SetWindowTitle
" ; "Backups done") ;

MBS
("
Dialog.Run

button =

("Dialog.GetButtonPressed")

examples.xml_code:

[

functions.name: Dialog.GetButtons
functions.description: Queries which buttons are shown. Possible combinations are: OK, OKCancel, RetryCancel, YesNo, YesNoCancel, AbortRetryIgnore or CancelTryContinue.

functions.name: Dialog.GetDefaultButton
functions.description: Queries the title for the default button.

functions.name: Dialog.GetFieldText
functions.description: Queries field text.

parameters.name: FieldIndex
parameters.description: The index of the field. Range from 0 to 9.

examples.label: Query text of first field:

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dialog.GetIcon
functions.description: Returns the icon displayed in the dialog. The default image is the application icon (NSApplicationIcon application property).

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG. Ignored on Windows: Always returns PNG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: Dialog.GetInformativeText
functions.description: Returns the receiver's informative text.

functions.name: Dialog.GetKeyEquivalent
functions.description: Queries the key equivalent for a button.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the button. From 0 to 9.

functions.name: Dialog.GetLastResult
functions.description: Provides the last result from the dialog. This is useful for sheets to query the status in a script later. If a timeout occurred, the result is "Timeout".

functions.name: Dialog.GetLeft
functions.description: Queries left position. Returns -1 if not defined.

functions.name: Dialog.GetMessage
functions.description: Queries the message text for the dialog.

functions.name: Dialog.GetMode
functions.description: Queries the dialog style.
functions.result: Returns the mode or the error.

functions.name: Dialog.GetOtherButton
functions.description: Queries the title for the other button.

functions.name: Dialog.GetSheetEvaluate
functions.description: Queries notification evaluation expression. If you use sheet functions you can setup an evaluation, so the plugin can evaluates this expression when sheet is done.

functions.name: Dialog.GetSheetFileName
functions.description: Queries notification script's file name. If you use sheet functions you can setup a file name and script name, so the plugin can call this script when sheet is done.

functions.name: Dialog.GetSheetRunning
functions.description: Queries if there is a sheet visible currently. You can run a loop with script pauses if you want your script to wait. You exit your loop when this function returns not a 1. This function is free to use. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 Dialog.GetSheetFileName - Dialog.GetSheetScriptName 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: Dialog.GetSheetScriptName
functions.description: Queries notification script name. If you use sheet functions you can setup a file name and script name, so the plugin can call this script when sheet is done.

functions.name: Dialog.GetShowsSuppressionButton
functions.description: Indicates whether the receiver shows a suppression button. Value is 1 when the alert shows a suppression button, 0 otherwise. The default is 0.

functions.name: Dialog.GetSuppressionButtonTitle
functions.description: Queries the text for the suppression checkbox.

functions.name: Dialog.GetSuppressionState
functions.description: Queries the suppression state after you showed the dialog. So first call Dialog.SetShowsSuppressionButton to set flag to 1. Then you use Dialog.Run to show the dialog and finally after the dialog is done, you can use this function to query result and see if checkbox was set by user.

functions.name: Dialog.GetTextAlignment
functions.description: Queries whether to change text alignment for dialog.

functions.name: Dialog.GetTimeout
functions.description: Queries the timeout for dialogs in seconds.

functions.name: Dialog.GetTitleIcon
functions.description: Returns the icon displayed in the titlebar.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG. Ignored on Windows: Always returns PNG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: Dialog.GetTop
functions.description: Queries top position. Returns -1 if not defined.

functions.name: Dialog.GetWindowTitle
functions.description: Queries the window title for the dialog.

functions.name: Dialog.Reset
functions.description: Resets all dialog options.

functions.name: Dialog.Run
functions.description: Runs the dialog. Returns which button was pressed. On Windows may return Abort, Cancel, Continue, Ignore, No, OK, Retry, Try Again or Yes. On Mac returns text of the buttons clicked. Version 6.2 adds new capabilities for Windows including custom buttons, timeout, suppression checkbox and icon.

functions.name: Dialog.RunSheet
functions.description: Runs dialog as sheet. Please pass reference to a window to use as parent window. The script will be triggered later and you can query the button clicked with Dialog.GetButtonPressed or Dialog.GetLastResult . The button clicked is also passed as parameter to the script. Use Dialog.SetSheetFileName and Dialog.SetSheetScriptName to define which script to run.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.name: Dialog.SetAlternateButton
functions.description: Sets the title for the alternate button.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new text.

functions.name: Dialog.SetButton
functions.description: Sets button text. Index 0 is default button, 1 other button, 2 alternate button and 3 to 9 are even more buttons.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the button. From 0 to 9.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new button label.

functions.name: Dialog.SetButtons
functions.description: Sets which buttons to show. On Windows only a fixed list of combinations are allowed. Possible combinations are: OK, OKCancel, RetryCancel, YesNo, YesNoCancel, AbortRetryIgnore or CancelTryContinue.

parameters.name: buttons
parameters.description: Which buttons to show.

functions.name: Dialog.SetDefaultButton
functions.description: Sets the title for the default button. When an empty string, a default localized button title ("OK" in English) is used.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new default button text.

parameters.name: NoDefault
parameters.description: Whether to not have a default button. Pass 1 to have no default button, so return key will not close dialog. Default value here is 0 for allowing enter. (Mac only)

functions.name: Dialog.SetFieldText
functions.description: Sets the text for a field.

parameters.name: FieldIndex
parameters.description: The index of the field. Range from 0 to 9.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text for the field.

functions.name: Dialog.SetIcon
functions.description: Sets the icon to be displayed in the alert to a given icon. By default on macOS, the image is the application icon, accessed through the application bundle's NSApplicationIcon property. So for FileMaker Pro, the icon is the FileMaker icon.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The container with image to use as dialog icon. Should be a PNG with transparency. Pass "" to clear icon.

functions.name: Dialog.SetInformativeText
functions.description: Sets the receiver's informative text to a given text. This text shows below the message text set with Dialog.SetMessage .

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new informative text.

functions.name: Dialog.SetKeyEquivalent
functions.description: Sets the key equivalent for a button. This allows you to set which button is activated with escape key (Char(27)), which is activated by return key (Char(13)) and which is activated by other characters.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the button. From 0 to 9.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new key equivalent.

examples.label: Set key equivalent for buttons:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Dialog.SetKeyEquivalent"; 0; Char(27)) // escape key ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Dialog.SetKeyEquivalent"; 1; Char(13)) // return key ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Dialog.SetKeyEquivalent"; 2; "e") // e key ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Dialog.SetKeyEquivalent"; 0; Char(27)) // escape key </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Dialog.SetKeyEquivalent"; 1; Char(13)) // return key </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Dialog.SetKeyEquivalent"; 2; "e") // e key </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dialog.SetLeft
functions.description: Sets the left position of the dialog. Value -1 means not defined. This is reset by Dialog.Reset function. You should not need this as dialog is automatically positioned in front of FileMaker window. See also Window.PositionNextDialog function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new horizontal position.

functions.name: Dialog.SetMessage
functions.description: Sets the message text for the dialog. Use Dialog.SetInformativeText to show some text in smaller font size below for information.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new message text.

functions.name: Dialog.SetMode
functions.description: Sets the dialog style. Indicates the severity level of the alert. Allowed modes on Mac: Warning, Information, Critical, Error. Allowed modes on Windows: Question, Hand, Stop, Error, Information, Warning or Exclamation. So the cross platform options are Information, Error and Warning. New in 7.1 for Mac: Note and Stop.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The new mode.

functions.name: Dialog.SetOtherButton
functions.description: Sets the title for the other button.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The title for the other button.

parameters.name: NoCancel
parameters.description: Whether to not allow a cancel button. Pass 1 to have no cancel button, so esc key will not close dialog. Default value here is 0 for allowing cancel. (Windows only)

functions.name: Dialog.SetSheetEvaluate
functions.description: Defines expression for the notification evaluation. If you run a sheet, this expression is evaluated when the sheet is done. Only used for Dialog.RunSheet .

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate.

functions.name: Dialog.SetSheetFileName
functions.description: Defines file name for the notification script. If you run a sheet, this file name is used to locate the script to call when the sheet is done. Only used for Dialog.RunSheet . With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

examples.label: Sets notification script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Dialog.SetSheetFileName"; "test.fmp12" )
MBS( "Dialog.SetSheetScriptName"; "SheetDoneScript" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dialog.SetSheetScriptName
functions.description: Defines script name for the notification script. If you run a sheet, this script name is used to locate the script to call when the sheet is done. Only used for Dialog.RunSheet . With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

examples.label: Sets notification script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Dialog.SetSheetFileName"; "test.fmp12" )
MBS( "Dialog.SetSheetScriptName"; "SheetDoneScript" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dialog.SetShowsSuppressionButton
functions.description: Specifies whether the receiver includes a suppression checkbox.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: When 1 the alert includes the suppression checkbox, else 0.

functions.name: Dialog.SetSuppressionButtonTitle
functions.description: Sets the text for the suppression checkbox. This allows you to show a checkbox. It doesn't need to be a suppression checkbox, you can use it for other questions to user.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new button label.

functions.name: Dialog.SetTextAlignment
functions.description: Sets whether to change text alignment for dialog. If you show multiple lines of text, it may be easier to read with left alignment.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass left, center, right or empty value for default.

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Dialog.SetTimeout

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new timeout value in seconds.

functions.name: Dialog.SetTitleIcon
functions.description: Sets the icon to be displayed in the window title to a given icon.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The container with image to use as dialog icon. Should be a PNG with transparency. Pass "" to clear icon.

functions.name: Dialog.SetTop
functions.description: Sets the top position of the dialog. Value -1 means not defined. This is reset by Dialog.Reset function. You should not need this as dialog is automatically positioned in front of FileMaker window. See also Window.PositionNextDialog function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new vertical position.

examples.label: Set to 100 points:

functions.name: Dialog.SetWindowTitle
functions.description: Sets the window title for the dialog. Only Windows operating system uses the window title property.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The new window title.

functions.name: FileDialog.GetAllowMulti
functions.description: Queries whether multiple selection is allowed.

functions.name: FileDialog.GetAllowsOtherFileTypes
functions.description: Queries if other file types.

functions.name: FileDialog.GetCanCreateDirectories
functions.description: Queries whether the user can create directories.

functions.name: FileDialog.GetCanSelectHiddenExtension
functions.description: Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the panel allows the user to hide or show extensions.
functions.result: Returns 1 when the panel allows the user to hide or show extensions; otherwise, 0.

functions.name: FileDialog.GetFilter
functions.description: Queries which filter is defined. Returns the values passed to FileDialog.SetFilter .

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to query. Can be MacFilter, WinFilter or WinLabels.

functions.name: FileDialog.GetInitialDirectory
functions.description: Queries the initial directory. Use Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath to convert to a FileMaker path if required.

functions.name: FileDialog.GetMessage
functions.description: Returns the message displayed in the save panel.

functions.name: FileDialog.GetNameFieldLabel
functions.description: Returns the string displayed in front of the filename text field.
functions.result: Returns initial text for the name field.

functions.name: FileDialog.GetNameFieldStringValue
functions.description: Returns the user-editable filename currently shown in the name field.

functions.name: FileDialog.GetPath
functions.description: Queries the native path with the given index. Use Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath to convert to a FileMaker path if required. Please note that in sheet mode ( FileDialog.SetUseSheet with 1), you need to wait for the trigger to be called before you can query paths.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the path.

examples.label: Export all containers to one folder:
examples.text_code:
#Ask for folder?
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
FileDialog.Reset
")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
FileDialog.SetMessage
"; "Export folder?")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
FileDialog.SelectFolderDialog
")]
If [
$r
= "OK"]
#get path from dialog:
Set Variable [
$npath
; Value:
MBS
( "FileDialog.GetPath"; 0 )]
#convert to FileMaker path for Export Field Contents
Set Variable [
$fpath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath
";
$npath
)]
#Loop over all records:
Go to Record/Request/Page [First]
Loop
#Export files from this record
#figure out file name
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:GetAsText (
Container Batch Export::Test1
)]
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:GetValue(
$name
; 1)]
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$name
)]
Set Variable [
$dpath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$fpath
;
$name
)]
Export Field Contents [
Container Batch Export::Test1
; “
$dpath
”]
#Next record
Go to Record/Request/Page [Next; Exit after last]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Ask for folder?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FileDialog.Reset")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FileDialog.SetMessage"; "Export folder?")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FileDialog.SelectFolderDialog")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$r = "OK"</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>get path from dialog:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FileDialog.GetPath"; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$npath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>convert to FileMaker path for Export Field Contents</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.NativePathToFilemakerPath"; $npath )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fpath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Loop over all records:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="First"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Export files from this record</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>figure out file name</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsText ( Container Batch Export::Test1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($name; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; $name )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $fpath; $name )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$dpath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="132" name="Export Field Contents">
    <AutoOpen state="False"/>
    <CreateEmail state="False"/>
    <Field table="Container Batch Export" id="99999" name="Test1"/>
    <UniversalPathList>$dpath</UniversalPathList>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Next record</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Exit state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="Next"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Select folder in one expression:

Let([
r =
MBS
("
FileDialog.Reset
");
r =
MBS
("
FileDialog.SelectFolderDialog
");
folder = If ( r = "OK" ;
MBS
("FileDialog.GetPath"; 0); "" )
]; folder)

r = MBS("FileDialog.Reset");
r = MBS("FileDialog.SelectFolderDialog");
folder = If ( r = "OK" ; MBS("FileDialog.GetPath"; 0); "" )

]; folder)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

File Path;

functions.name: FileDialog.Reset
functions.description: Resets all options to default values. Clears all text values and sets boolean values to false except ResolvesAliases which is set to true.

examples.label: Resets file dialog options:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FileDialog.Reset")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FileDialog.GetPathCount")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("FileDialog.GetPath"; $index)</Calculation>
    <Field table="FileDialog" id="99999" name="Path"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$index = $count</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Export all containers to one folder:
examples.text_code:
#Ask for folder?
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
FileDialog.Reset
")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
FileDialog.SetMessage
"; "Export folder?")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("FileDialog.SelectFolderDialog")]
If [
$r
= "OK"]
#get path from dialog:
Set Variable [
$npath
; Value:
MBS
( "
FileDialog.GetPath
"; 0 )]
#convert to FileMaker path for Export Field Contents
Set Variable [
$fpath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath
";
$npath
)]
#Loop over all records:
Go to Record/Request/Page [First]
Loop
#Export files from this record
#figure out file name
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:GetAsText (
Container Batch Export::Test1
)]
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:GetValue(
$name
; 1)]
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$name
)]
Set Variable [
$dpath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$fpath
;
$name
)]
Export Field Contents [
Container Batch Export::Test1
; “
$dpath
”]
#Next record
Go to Record/Request/Page [Next; Exit after last]
End Loop
End If

functions.name: FileDialog.SetAllowMulti
functions.description: Sets whether multiple selection is allowed. Default is disallowed (0). Selecting folder may not allow multiple selection. But selection files or items may do.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: Whether to allow multiple selection. Set to 0 to disallow and 1 to allow.

examples.label: Allow multi file selection:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FileDialog.SetAllowMulti"; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FileDialog.SetAllowsOtherFileTypes
functions.description: Sets whether other file types are allowed. Only for save panel to allow saving in other file types. Added Windows support in plugin version 8.3 for Windows Vista or newer.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable other file types or 0 to disable.

examples.label: Allow other file types:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FileDialog.SetAllowsOtherFileTypes"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FileDialog.SetCanCreateDirectories
functions.description: Sets whether to allow directory creation. By default we allow it in save dialogs and disable it in open dialogs.

parameters.name: Value

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FileDialog.SetFilter
functions.description: Queries whether other file types are allowed. Mac filter works in FileMaker 12 or newer, but seems not to work in FileMaker 11. If you like to select any file, please don't define a filter at all.

parameters.name: MacFilter
parameters.description: The filter specification for Mac. Can be file extension (Without dot) or UTI (Uniform Type Identifier). Can be a list.

parameters.name: WinFilter
parameters.description: The filter specification for Windows. This is normally star dot file extension and can be a list.

parameters.name: WinLabels
parameters.description: The list of labels for the file types.

examples.label: Set Mac filter with file extension as filter
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FileDialog.SetFilter"; "jpg"; "*.JPG"; "JPEG files")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Set Mac filter with UTI as filter:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FileDialog.SetFilter"; "public.jpeg"; "*.JPG"; "JPEG files")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FileDialog.SetPrompt

By default, the text in the default button is "Open" for an Open panel and "Save" for a Save panel. It is intended that short words or phrases, such as "Open", "Save", "Set", or "Choose", be used on the button. The button is not resized to accommodate long prompts. Added Windows support in plugin version 8.3 for Windows Vista or newer.

functions.name: IBAN.CalcCheckSum

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IBAN.Compact

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IBAN.Countries
functions.description: Queries list of country codes. The plugin includes a list of over 100 country codes.

examples.label: Query examples for each country:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of countries
	list = MBS( "IBAN.Countries" ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	Country = GetValue(list; index); 
	// query example for each country
	example = MBS( "IBAN.Example"; Country ); 
	names = names & Country & " -> " & example & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IBAN.Example
functions.description: Queries example IBAN for country.

parameters.name: Country
parameters.description: Pass country code.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of countries
	list = MBS( "IBAN.Countries" ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	Country = GetValue(list; index); 
	// query example for each country
	example = MBS( "IBAN.Example"; Country ); 
	names = names & Country & " -> " & example & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IBAN.IsValid
functions.description: Checks whether an IBAN is valid. Ignores invalid characters in input, e.g. strip out dots, spaces or dashes. The IBAN must: have a valid country code. have the minimum length for the country. match the structure of the country's IBAN format (See IBAN.RegEx ) have a valid checksum. Returns 1 if valid or 0 if not. You may want to do a trim() on the number yourself.

parameters.name: IBAN
parameters.description: The IBAN to check.

examples.label: Check whether IBAN is valid:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("IBAN.isValid"; "DE29100100100987654321")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IBAN.RegEx
functions.description: Queries regular expression for country. You may use the regular expression to find IBAN within text.

parameters.name: Country
parameters.description: Pass country code.

examples.label: Query RegEx for Germany:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "IBAN.RegEx"; "DE" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find a german IBAN in a text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
Input = "Hello DE29100100100987654321 World!";
R = MBS( "IBAN.RegEx"; "DE");
R = substitute(R; "^"; "");
R = substitute(R; "$"; "");

Found = MBS("RegEx.Extract"; input; R; "\\0"; 0 )
]; Found)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IsWindows
functions.description: Whether we run on Windows. Returns 1 if running on Windows and 0 if not. See also IsMacOS , IsLinux and IsIOS .

examples.label: Register LibXL for MBS Plugin
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("
XL.IsInitialized
") ≠ 1 ]
If [
MBS
("IsMacOS") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Initialize
";
$path
; "xxx"; "mac-xxx" )]
Else If [
MBS
("IsWindows") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Initialize
";
$path
; "xxx"; "win-xxx" )]
Else If [
MBS
("IsLinux") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Initialize
";
$path
; "xxx"; "linux-xxx" )]
Else If [
MBS
("IsIOS") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
XL.Initialize
";
$path
; "xxx"; "ios-xxx" )]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value: "new platform?" ]
End If
If [
$r
≠ "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Problem with LibXL Registration";
$r
]

End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS("XL.IsInitialized") ≠ 1 ]
   If [ MBS("IsMacOS") ]
      Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Initialize"; $path; "xxx"; "mac-xxx" )]
   Else If [ MBS("IsWindows") ]
      Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Initialize"; $path; "xxx"; "win-xxx" )]
   Else If [ MBS("IsLinux") ]
      Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Initialize"; $path; "xxx"; "linux-xxx" )]
   Else If [ MBS("IsIOS") ]
      Set Variable [ $r; Value: MBS( "XL.Initialize"; $path; "xxx"; "ios-xxx" )]
   Else
      Set Variable [ $r; Value: "new platform?" ]
   End If
   If [$r ≠ "OK"]
      Show Custom Dialog ["Problem with LibXL Registration"; $r]
   End If
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MenuItem.CreateMenuItem
functions.description: Creates a new menu item. You later need to release it with MenuItem.Release . Menu item reference numbers are starting at 11000 and counting up for each new menu item.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title of the menu item.

parameters.name: keyEquivalent
parameters.description: Optional, a string representing a keyboard key to be used as the key equivalent.

functions.result: Returns menu item reference number or error.

examples.label: Show a context menu based on a table with menu entries:
examples.text_code:
# go to layout with menu entries
Go to Layout [ “REP” (REP Reports) ]
# this script can be called with various groups
Set Variable [
$type
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
# make a new menu
Set Variable [
$menu
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menu.CreateMenu
") ]
# loop over records
Go to Record/Request/Page [ First ]
Loop
# if group matches
If [
REP Reports::Group
=
$type
]
# add new menu item with title from table
Set Variable [
$item
; Value:
MBS
( "MenuItem.CreateMenuItem";
REP Reports::LabelReport
) ]
# define which script to call if menu item is selected
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MenuItem.SetScriptAction
";
$item
; Get(FileName);
REP Reports::Script
) ]
# add item to menu
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menu.AddItem
";
$menu
;
$item
) ]
End If
# next record?
Go to Record/Request/Page [ Next ; Exit after last ]
End Loop
# switch layout back
Go to Layout [ original layout ]
# Show menu
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menu.PopUp
";
$menu
; "mouse") ]
# Cleanup memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menu.Release
";
$menu
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> go to layout with menu entries</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="REP"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> this script can be called with various groups</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$type</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> make a new menu</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.CreateMenu") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$menu</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over records</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="First"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> if group matches</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> REP Reports::Group = $type </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add new menu item with title from table</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MenuItem.CreateMenuItem"; REP Reports::LabelReport) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$item</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> define which script to call if menu item is selected</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MenuItem.SetScriptAction"; $item; Get(FileName); REP Reports::Script ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add item to menu</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.AddItem"; $menu; $item ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next record?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Exit state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="Next"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> switch layout back</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="OriginalLayout"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Show menu</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.PopUp"; $menu; "mouse") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menu.Release"; $menu) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetEnabled
functions.description: Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the receiver is enabled. See also MenuItem.SetEnabled .

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetEvaluate
functions.description: Queries expression to be run when MenuItem is chosen.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetFontColor
functions.description: Queries font color for menu item. Color is return as # encoded hex number, e.g. #FF0000 for red.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetFontName
functions.description: Queries font name to use. Empty means system font.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetFontSize
functions.description: Queries font size to use for menu item. Font size 0 is to use system default size.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetGroupID
functions.description: Queries the group id of the menu item. If several menu items have the same group id, the plugin only shows the first one fulfilling all requirements like key modifiers.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetHidden
functions.description: Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the menu item is hidden. 1 if the menu item is hidden, otherwise 0. Available in OS X v10.5 and later. See also MenuItem.SetHidden .

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetImage
functions.description: Queries the image for this menu item. See also MenuItem.SetImage . Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for this image.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: MenuItem.GetItalic
functions.description: Queries if menu item should use italic font style.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetKeyEquivalent
functions.description: Returns the menuitem's unmodified keyboard equivalent. The menuitem's unmodified keyboard equivalent, or the empty string if one hasn't been defined. Use MenuItem.GetKeyEquivalentModifierMask to determine the modifier mask for the key equivalent. See also MenuItem.SetKeyEquivalent .

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetKeyEquivalentModifierMask
functions.description: Returns the receiver's keyboard equivalent modifier mask.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetMixedStateImage
functions.description: Returns the image used to depict a mixed state. A mixed state is useful for indicating a mix of "off" and "on" attribute values in a group of selected objects, such as a selection of text containing boldface and plain (non-boldface) words. By default this is a horizontal line. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for this image.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: MenuItem.GetOffStateImage
functions.description: Returns the image used to depict the receiver's off state. The image used to depict the receiver's off state, or empty if the image has not been set. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for this image.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: MenuItem.GetOnStateImage
functions.description: Returns the image used to depict the receiver's on state. The image used to depict the receiver's "on" state, or empty if the image has not been set. Please call GMImage.Release later to free memory for this image.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: MenuItem.GetParentItem
functions.description: Returns the menu item whose submenu contains the menu item. The parent menu item, or empty text if the receiver does not have a parent item.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetParentMenu
functions.description: Returns the menu containing the menu item. The parent menu, or empty text if the item does not have a parent item.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.result: Returns menu reference number or error.

functions.name: MenuItem.GetPath
functions.description: Queries path of a menu item in a menu hierarchy.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Can be Title, Tag or ID and defines which value to use.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: Which delimiter to use. Default is newline.

functions.name: MenuItem.GetRequireCommand

parameters.name: Item

functions.name: MenuItem.GetRequireControl
functions.description: Queries if this menu item only shows if control key is pressed.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetRequireOption

functions.name: MenuItem.GetRequireShift
functions.description: Queries if this menu item only shows if shift key is pressed.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

functions.name: MenuItem.GetScriptFileName

functions.name: MenuItem.GetScriptName

parameters.name: Item

functions.name: MenuItem.GetTag
functions.description: Queries the tag value of the menu. See also Menu.SetTag . With the tag, we allow you to associate a text with the menu item, for example a record ID.

parameters.name: Item

examples.label: Show menu and show selection:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$menu
; Value:
MBS
("
Menu.CreateMenu
")]
# create menu items...
Set Variable [
$item
; Value:
MBS
("
MenuItem.CreateMenuItem
"; "Hello World")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
MenuItem.SetTag
";
$item
; "secret value")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Menu.AddItem
";
$menu
;
$item
)]
#
#show menu
Set Variable [
$m
; Value:
MBS
("
Menu.PopUp
";
$Menu
; "mouse")]
#
#you selected something?
If [
$m
= 1]
Set Variable [
$selectedItem
; Value:
MBS
("
Menu.SelectedItem
";
$menu
)]
Set Variable [
$selectedTitle
; Value:
MBS
("
MenuItem.GetTitle
";
$selectedItem
)]
Set Variable [
$selectedTag
; Value:
MBS
("MenuItem.GetTag";
$selectedItem
)]
#
Show Custom Dialog ["Auswahl";
$selectedItem
& ¶ &
$selectedTitle
& ¶ &
$selectedTag
]
End If
Set Variable [
$m
; Value:
MBS
("
Menu.Release
";
$Menu
; 1)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Menu.CreateMenu")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$menu</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> create menu items...</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("MenuItem.CreateMenuItem"; "Hello World")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$item</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("MenuItem.SetTag"; $item; "secret value")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Menu.AddItem"; $menu; $item)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>show menu</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Menu.PopUp"; $Menu; "mouse")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$m</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>you selected something?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$m = 1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Menu.SelectedItem"; $menu)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$selectedItem</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("MenuItem.GetTitle"; $selectedItem)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$selectedTitle</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("MenuItem.GetTag"; $selectedItem)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$selectedTag</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Auswahl"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$selectedItem & ¶ &  $selectedTitle & ¶ &  $selectedTag</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Menu.Release"; $Menu; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$m</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MenuItem.IsSeparatorItem

functions.name: MenuItem.List
functions.description: Queries list of all IDs for menu item objects. You may use the list to loop over the entries and apply some styles to all.

functions.name: MenuItem.Release
functions.description: Releases the menu item. You need to release menu items after you finished using it to avoid running out of memory.

parameters.name: Item

parameters.name: children
parameters.description: If 1, releases all child menu items and child menus.

functions.name: MenuItem.ReleaseAll

functions.name: MenuItem.SetAlternate
functions.description: Marks the receiver as an alternate to the previous menu item. If the receiver has the same key equivalent as the previous item, but has different key equivalent modifiers, the items are folded into a single visible item and the appropriate item shows while tracking the menu, depending on what modifier key (if any) is pressed. The menu items may also have no key equivalent as long as the key equivalent modifiers are different. See also MenuItem.GetAlternate .

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: 1 if the receiver is an alternate to the previous menu item, 0 otherwise.

functions.name: MenuItem.SetAttributedTitle
functions.description: Applies a formatted text to the menu item. Supported styles are bold, italic, font name, font size, underline and colors. See also MenuItem.GetAttributedTitle .

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text with styles.

functions.name: MenuItem.SetBackColor
functions.description: Sets the back color of the menu item. Currently only used on Windows. On Mac it would only color the area where the text is and not the whole menu item, so this feature is disabled for Mac.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 255. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

examples.label: Creates a menu item for background color:

$item
; Value:

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

("MenuItem.SetBackColor";
$item
; "#FF0000")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Menu.AddItem
";
$menu
;
$item
)]

functions.name: MenuItem.SetEvaluate
functions.description: Sets expression to be run when menu item is chosen. In the expression the text $$ID$$ will be replaced when evaluated with the hotkey reference, so you can pass it to other functions if needed.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: MenuItem.SetFontColor
functions.description: Sets the font color to use. On Windows, a few features are not compatible with having a custom color.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a string in the following format "HSL h s l a", "YUV y u v a", "RGB r g b a", "MONO m a", "GRAY g a" or "COLOR R G B a". Alpha value a is optional. Range of values is 0 to 1.0 except if you use COLOR where RGB values are 0 to 255. Starting with version 5.4 of our plugin, you can use hex notation. # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

)]

examples.label: Loop over all menuitems in a menu and change font size:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While ( 
[
  // get items in menu 
  items = MBS( "Menu.Items"; $menu ); 
  index = 1;
  count = MBS( "Menu.NumberOfItems"; $menu )
] ; 

// loop till last item
index ≤ count ; 

[ 
  // get the current item
  item = GetValue(items; index);
  // change font size
  r = MBS( "MenuItem.SetFontSize"; item; 16 ); 
  // and continue with next item
  index = index + 1
] ; 0 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MenuItem.SetKeyEquivalent
functions.description: Sets the receiver's unmodified key equivalent. This method considers the case of the letter passed to determine if it has a Shift modifier added. That is, "w" sets the key equivalent to Command-w, while "W" is Command-Shift-w. You use MenuItem.SetKeyEquivalentModifierMask to set the appropriate mask for the modifier keys for the key equivalent.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: A string containing a character code representing a keyboard key. If you want to remove the key equivalent from a menu item, pass an empty string.

examples.label: Add menu item with key ⇧⌘T:
examples.text_code:

Set Variable [
$ShiftKeyMask
; Value:131072]
Set Variable [
$ControlKeyMask
; Value:262144]
Set Variable [
$AlternateKeyMask
; Value:524288]
Set Variable [
$CommandKeyMask
; Value:1048576]
#now make menu entry
Set Variable [
$item
; Value:
MBS
("
MenuItem.CreateMenuItem
"; "With Key Equivalent")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("MenuItem.SetKeyEquivalent";
$item
; "T")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
MenuItem.SetKeyEquivalentModifierMask
";
$item
;
$ShiftKeyMask
+
$CommandKeyMask
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Menu.AddItem
";
$menu
;
$item
)]
