functions.name: MenuItem.SetKeyEquivalentModifierMask
functions.description: Sets the menu items's keyboard equivalent modifiers. In general, you are strongly encouraged to always set NSCommandKeyMask in mask, although there may be some conventions where this is not required. For example, in an application that plays media, the Play command may be mapped to just " " (space), without the command key. NSShiftKeyMask is a valid modifier for any key equivalent in mask. This allows you to specify key-equivalents such as Command-Shift-1 that are consistent across all keyboards. However, with a few exceptions (such as the German "ß" character), a lowercase character with NSShiftKeyMask is interpreted the same as the uppercase character without that mask. For example, Command-Shift-c and Command-C are considered to be identical key equivalents.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Mask
parameters.description: The key masks indicate modifiers such as the Shift or Option keys. mask is a number and you can add the constants below.

; Value:262144]

; Value:524288]

; Value:1048576]
#now make menu entry

$item
; Value:
MBS
("
MenuItem.CreateMenuItem
"; "With Key Equivalent")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
MenuItem.SetKeyEquivalent
";
$item
; "T")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("MenuItem.SetKeyEquivalentModifierMask";
$item
;
$ShiftKeyMask
+
$CommandKeyMask
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Menu.AddItem
";
$menu
;
$item
)]

functions.name: MenuItem.SetScriptAction
functions.description: Sets which script is called if a menu item in this menu is selected. You can use MenuItem.SetScriptAction to associate a script with individual items or use this function for a whole menu. See also MenuItem.GetScriptFileName and MenuItem.GetScriptName . With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename of file which contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script.

parameters.name: ScriptParameter
parameters.description: If you want a specific script parameter. Default is to leave empty and get the ID fo the menu item. You can store a value with the menu item via MenuItem.SetTag .

$item
; Value:
MBS
("
MenuItem.CreateMenuItem
"; "Hello World")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("MenuItem.SetScriptAction";
$item
; Get(FileName); "MenuScript")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Menu.AddItem
";
$menu
;
$item
)]

functions.name: MenuItem.SetState
functions.description: Sets the state of the menu item. The image associated with the new state is displayed to the left of the menu item. See also MenuItem.GetState . Mixed state is not supported on Windows. On Mac the special values "bullet" and "diamond" sets state to on and switch to use bullet/diamond images.

parameters.name: Item

parameters.name: itemState
parameters.description: The new state; it should be one of Off, on or mixed.

functions.name: MenuItem.SetSubMenu
functions.description: Sets the submenu of the menu item. See also MenuItem.GetSubMenu .

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: submenu
parameters.description: The reference to the menu. Please create one with Menu.CreateMenu .

functions.name: MenuItem.SetTag
functions.description: Sets the tag of the menu. See also Menu.GetTag . With the tag, we allow you to associate a text with the menu item, for example a record ID.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new tag value.

functions.name: MenuItem.SetTitle
functions.description: Sets the menuitem's title. See also See also MenuItem.GetTitle .

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new title of the menu item. If you do not want a title, use an empty string.

functions.name: MenuItem.SetToolTip

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The reference to the menu item. Please create one with MenuItem.CreateMenuItem or MenuItem.CreateSeparator .

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: Optional, a short string that describes the menu item.

functions.name: PDFKit.AddEmptyPage
functions.description: Adds an empty page to the given PDF document. Your changes are only done in memory, so please remember to write modified pdf back to disk, e.g. by using PDFKit.WriteToPath . Or use PDFKit.GetPDFDocument to get a PDF for storing in a container.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A PDF reference number from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The width for the new PDF page.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The height of the new PDF page.

examples.label: Create PDF from images:
examples.text_code:
# Start new document
Set Variable [
$doc
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.NewPDFDocument
")]
# Add image in full size as new page
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.AddImagePage
";
$doc
;
test::image
)]
# Add empty page in default size
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("PDFKit.AddEmptyPage";
$doc
)]
# Add image in given size as new page:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.AddImagePage
";
$doc
;
test::image
; 400; 300)]
# get PDF document and store in container:
Set Field [
test::test
;
MBS
("
PDFKit.GetPDFDocument
";
$doc
)]
# release memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$doc
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Start new document</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.NewPDFDocument")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$doc</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add image in full size as new page</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.AddImagePage"; $doc; test::image)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add empty page in default size</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.AddEmptyPage"; $doc)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add image in given size as new page:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.AddImagePage"; $doc; test::image; 400; 300)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get PDF document and store in container:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.GetPDFDocument"; $doc)</Calculation>
    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="test"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $doc)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Loop over PDF documents in records and import them into a new PDF and add empty pages between:
examples.text_code:
Go to Record/Request/Page [ First ]
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.NewPDFDocument
" ) ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.AppendPages
";
$pdf
;
Merge PDFs::InputPDF
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "PDFKit.AddEmptyPage";
$pdf
; 700; 500) ]
Go to Record/Request/Page [ Next ; Exit after last: On ]
End Loop
Set Field [
Merge PDFs::FinalPDF
;
MBS
("
PDFKit.GetPDFDocument
";
$pdf
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$pdf
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="First"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.NewPDFDocument" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.AppendPages"; $pdf; Merge PDFs::InputPDF) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.AddEmptyPage"; $pdf; 700; 500) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Exit state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="Next"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.GetPDFDocument"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Merge PDFs" id="99999" name="FinalPDF "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.AddImageFilePage
functions.description: Adds a new page to PDF document with given image file. If you need more control about details, you may want to check DynaPDF functions. Your changes are only done in memory, so please remember to write modified pdf back to disk, e.g. by using PDFKit.WriteToPath . Or use PDFKit.GetPDFDocument to get a PDF for storing in a container. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A PDF reference number from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The file path to the image file to use.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The width for the new PDF page.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The height of the new PDF page.

examples.label: Create PDF from image file:

; Value:

Set Variable [
$r

$ref

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.WriteToPath
";
$ref
; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.pdf")]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$ref
)]

functions.name: PDFKit.AddImagePage

Please use PDFKit.AddImageFilePage to add image file on disk. Your changes are only done in memory, so please remember to write modified pdf back to disk, e.g. by using PDFKit.WriteToPath . Or use PDFKit.GetPDFDocument to get a PDF for storing in a container.

parameters.name: PDF

parameters.name: Image

parameters.name: Width

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The height of the new PDF page.

    <Text> release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $doc)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.AppendPages
functions.description: Copies pages from one PDF to append other PDF. Your changes are only done in memory, so please remember to write modified pdf back to disk, e.g. by using PDFKit.WriteToPath . Or use PDFKit.GetPDFDocument to get a PDF for storing in a container.

parameters.name: PDFRef
parameters.description: The PDF Reference where pages are added.

parameters.name: OtherPDFs
parameters.description: Several PDF references. A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

functions.result: Returns OK on success or error on failure.

examples.label: Merge two PDFs:
examples.text_code:

$pdf
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.OpenContainer
";
MyTable::InputPDF
) ]
Set Variable [

$pdf
;
MyTable::OtherPDF
) ]

";
$PDF
) ]
Set Variable [

; Value:

( "
PDFKit.Release
";
$pdf
) ]

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Loop over PDF documents in records and import them into a new PDF and add empty pages between:

$pdf
; Value:

("
PDFKit.Release
";
$pdf

functions.name: PDFKit.Combine
functions.description: Creates a new PDF file with all the given PDF files/references. You can pass container values, URLs or PDF references. You can pass one PDF reference to save to a new PDF. Please note the difference between PDFKit.Combine and PDFKit.CombineRef . Combine returns a new container value with the new PDF. CombineRef returns the new PDF reference for further processing. If on combining one of the PDF parameters is not a valid PDF, the function returns an error. You can use PDFKit.SetIgnoreCombineErrors to disable that and ignore all PDFs which fail to open. For example if you write code which takes 4 container values and not all of them contain PDF files. Starting with version 6.0, the PDFs parameters can also be lists of paths or reference numbers. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat PDFs parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: A text with the file name to include with the PDF.

parameters.name: PDFs...
parameters.description: Several PDF references. A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open . The function will fail if one of those is an image file.

examples.label: Combine two PDF files form two media fields into a new PDF
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Combine"; "test.pdf"; mytable::test1; mytable::test2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Merge PDFs:

#Start with blank list
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:""]
#Load PDF from container
Go to Record/Request/Page [First]
Set Variable [
$destPage
; Value:1]
Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.OpenContainer
";
Merge PDFs::InputPDF
)]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0]
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
$list
&
$r
& ¶]
End If
Go to Record/Request/Page [Next; Exit after last]
End Loop
Set Field [
Merge PDFs::FinalPDF
;
MBS
("PDFKit.Combine"; "merge.pdf";
$list
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$list
)]

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="First"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$destPage</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.OpenContainer"; Merge PDFs::InputPDF)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError") = 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$list & $r & ¶</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Exit state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="Next"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Combine"; "merge.pdf"; $list)</Calculation>
    <Field table="Merge PDFs" id="99999" name="FinalPDF"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $list)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Merge PDFs with various paths:

functions.name: PDFKit.CombineRef
functions.description: Creates a new PDF document with all the given PDF files/references and returns the new PDF reference. You can pass container values, URLs or PDF references. You can pass one PDF reference to save to a new PDF. Please note the difference between PDFKit.Combine and PDFKit.CombineRef. Combine returns a new container value with the new PDF. CombineRef returns the new PDF reference for further processing. If on combining one of the PDF parameters is not a valid PDF, the function returns an error. You can use PDFKit.SetIgnoreCombineErrors to disable that and ignore all PDFs which fail to open. For example if you write code which takes 4 container values and not all of them contain PDF files. Starting with version 6.0, the PDFs parameters can also be lists of paths or reference numbers. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat PDFs parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: A text with the file name to include with the PDF.

parameters.name: PDFs...
parameters.description: Several PDF references. A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

examples.label: Combine the PDF files in the three PDF references into a new PDF reference:

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.CombineRef"; ""; $ref1; $ref2; $ref3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.GeneratePreview
functions.description: Adds preview to an existing PDF container. For Windows or Linux, please use DynaPDF.GeneratePreview . See Plugin.SetPreviewSize for setting resolution of previews. If called on Windows, will internally run WinPDF.GeneratePreview function.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: A media value containing PDF data.

functions.result: Returns updated container value or error.

examples.label: Generate preview:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.GeneratePreview"; Merge PDFs::InputPDF) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Merge PDFs" id="99999" name="FinalPDF "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add a preview to PDF container if needed:
examples.text_code:
# get a PDF somewhere
Set Variable [
$PDF
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.ReadFile
"; "C:\Users\Christian\Desktop\test.pdf"; "auto") ]
# Does have PDF and no image?
Set Variable [
$types
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.GetTypes
";
$PDF
) ]
# PDF included?
If [ Position (
$types
; "PDF " ; 1 ; 1 ) > 0 ]
# Image preview missing?
If [ Position (
$types
; "JPEG" ; 1 ; 1 ) < 1 and Position (
$types
; "PNGf" ; 1 ; 1 ) < 1 ]
# Use DynaPDF if initialized, we can use it
If [
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.IsInitialized
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$PDF2
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.GeneratePreview
";
$PDF
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
# Success, so use the new PDF
Set Variable [
$PDF
; Value:
$PDF2
]
End If
Else If [
MBS
( "IsMacOSX" ) = 1 ]
# On Mac use PDFKit
Set Variable [
$PDF2
; Value:
MBS
( "PDFKit.GeneratePreview";
$PDF
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
# Success, so use the new PDF
Set Variable [
$PDF
; Value:
$PDF2
]
End If
End If
End If
End If
# Store PDF with preview
Set Field [
test::Image
;
$PDF
]

functions.name: PDFKit.GetCurrentFilter
functions.description: Queries name of current selected filter.

functions.name: PDFKit.GetIgnoreCombineErrors
functions.description: Returns status of Combine Error handling. Returns true if errors should be ignored or false if not.
functions.result: Returns the current value of the ignore combine error status.

functions.name: PDFKit.GetPDFAttribute
functions.description: Queries a PDF attribute. New keys in v5.4: OwnerPassword, UserPassword, EncryptionKeyLength, AllowsPrinting and AllowsCopying.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: Which information to query. This can be one of the following: Title, Subject, Author, Creator, Producer, Keywords, CreationDate or ModificationDate.

functions.result: Returns the text or date value requested.

examples.label: Get all the information values for a PDF in a container:
examples.text_code:
"Title: " &
MBS
("PDFKit.GetPDFAttribute";
combine pdf::test1
; "Title")  & "¶" &
"Subject: " &
MBS
("PDFKit.GetPDFAttribute";
combine pdf::test1
; "Subject")  & "¶" &
"Author: " &
MBS
("PDFKit.GetPDFAttribute";
combine pdf::test1
; "Author")  & "¶" &
"Creator: " &
MBS
("PDFKit.GetPDFAttribute";
combine pdf::test1
; "Creator")  & "¶" &
"Producer: " &
MBS
("PDFKit.GetPDFAttribute";
combine pdf::test1
; "Producer")  & "¶" &
"Keywords: " &
MBS
("PDFKit.GetPDFAttribute";
combine pdf::test1
; "Keywords")  & "¶" &
"CreationDate: " &

functions.name: PDFKit.GetPDFDocument
functions.description: Creates a new PDF Document with the given PDF document. Filename is optional. When you use an URL for the PDF, you can basically load a new PDF from a server. See Plugin.SetPreviewSize to control size of preview picture.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: Optionally, the file name to use for the container value.

functions.result: Returns a container with a PDF document. The plugin adds a JPEG picture for preview as well as a file name. You can store it in a media field.

examples.label: Save a PDF in a PDF reference into a container value:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.GetPDFDocument"; $ref; "test.pdf" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create PDF from images:
examples.text_code:
# Start new document
Set Variable [
$doc
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.NewPDFDocument
")]
# Add image in full size as new page
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.AddImagePage
";
$doc
;
test::image
)]
# Add empty page in default size
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.AddEmptyPage
";
$doc
)]
# Add image in given size as new page:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.AddImagePage
";
$doc
;
test::image
; 400; 300)]
# get PDF document and store in container:
Set Field [
test::test
;
MBS
("PDFKit.GetPDFDocument";
$doc
)]
# release memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$doc
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Start new document</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.NewPDFDocument")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$doc</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add image in full size as new page</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.AddImagePage"; $doc; test::image)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add empty page in default size</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.AddEmptyPage"; $doc)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add image in given size as new page:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.AddImagePage"; $doc; test::image; 400; 300)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get PDF document and store in container:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.GetPDFDocument"; $doc)</Calculation>
    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="test"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $doc)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage
functions.signature: MBS( "PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage"; PDF; Index { ; ImageType; FileName; DPI; Box; ClearBackground } )
functions.description: Renders an image of a page in a PDF document. With plugin version 2.4 you can specify format with ImageType parameter. Due to bugs in Apple’s PDFKit you may see memory leaks with JPEG data from PDF pages (seen in macOS 10.12). For Windows, Linux or iOS, please check DynaPDF.RenderPage or DynaPDF.GeneratePreview function. If you only need Windows, please check WinPDF.PageImage function. See Plugin.SetPreviewSize to control size of preview picture.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the page. From zero to PDFKit.GetPDFPageCount -1.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

parameters.name: DPI
parameters.description: The DPI to use for the resolution of the image. Default is 72.

parameters.name: Box
parameters.description: The PDF box to use. Can be Media Box = 0, Crop Box = 1, Bleed Box = 2, TrimBox = 3 or ArtBox = 4. If the box is not defined, we fall back to the media box. Default is 0.

parameters.name: ClearBackground
parameters.description: Optionally, whether to have a transparent background (1) vs. a white background (0). Default is 0 for white. For JPEG, you can't pass 1.

functions.result: Returns a container with a JPEG picture of the PDF page. You can store it in a media field.

examples.label: Render page 2 of a PDF into a picture
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage"; $ref; 1; "jpeg"; "test.jpg"; 300; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Make preview of page:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("IsWindows") ]
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.LoadContainer
";
Get Preview::Input
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$NewImage
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.PageImageAtSize
";
$pdf
; 0; 0; 600; "JPEG"; "page.jpg") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Preview
;
$NewImage
]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Made
using ; "WinPDF" ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.Release
";
$pdf
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.Release
";
$pdf
) ]
End If
Else If [
MBS
("IsMacOS") or
MBS
("IsIOS") ]
Set Variable [
$preview
; Value:
MBS
( "PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage";
$Input
; 0; "JPEG"; "input.jpg"; 72) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Preview
;
$preview
]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Made
using ; "PDFKit" ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS("IsWindows") ] 
	Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS( "WinPDF.LoadContainer"; Get Preview::Input) ] 
	If [ MBS("IsError") = 0 ] 
		Set Variable [ $NewImage ; Value: MBS( "WinPDF.PageImageAtSize"; $pdf; 0; 0; 600; "JPEG"; "page.jpg") ] 
		If [ MBS("IsError") = 0 ] 
			Set Field [ Get Preview::Preview ; $NewImage ] 
			Set Field [ Get Preview::Made using ; "WinPDF" ] 
			Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "WinPDF.Release"; $pdf) ] 
			Exit Script [ Text Result:    ] 
		End If
		Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "WinPDF.Release"; $pdf) ] 
	End If
Else If [ MBS("IsMacOS") or MBS("IsIOS") ] 
	Set Variable [ $preview ; Value: MBS( "PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage"; $Input; 0; "JPEG"; "input.jpg"; 72) ] 
	If [ MBS("IsError") = 0 ] 
		Set Field [ Get Preview::Preview ; $preview ] 
		Set Field [ Get Preview::Made using ; "PDFKit" ] 
		Exit Script [ Text Result:    ] 
	End If
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.GetPDFPageRotation
functions.description: Returns the page rotation angle in degrees. The rotation is a positive multiple of 90: 0, 90, 180, or 270. The rotation of pages with negative rotation is converted to a corresponding positive rotation.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the page. From zero to PDFKit.GetPDFPageCount -1.

functions.name: PDFKit.GetPDFPageText
functions.description: Queries the text of a page in a PDF document. If you need text from all pages, please use PDFKit.GetPDFText . For solutions on Windows, Linux or iOS, please use DynaPDF.ExtractPageText . You may need to use Text.ConvertUnicodeToCharacterComposition if text is coming back in decomposed unicode characters. For DynaPDF.ExtractText we sort text blocks, but for PDFKit functions we can just get the text in the order it appears in the PDF, independent of its position. Text is there for indexing or search, but not in the order you would read it.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the page. From zero to PDFKit.GetPDFPageCount -1.

functions.result: The text of the PDF page as far as PDFKit knows it.

functions.name: PDFKit.GetPrintLastSettings
functions.description: Queries current print settings. For user to read. To save settings, you can use PDFKit.SetPrintSetting and PDFKit.GetPrintLastSettings. Since version 10.1 we return settings as JSON instead of XML and includes print parameter.

examples.label: Queries last print settings:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$v
; Value:
MBS
( "PDFKit.GetPrintLastSettings") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Set Field [
PDFKit Print::Result
;
$v
]
Else
Set Field [
PDFKit Print::Settings
;
$v
]
Set Field [
PDFKit Print::Result
; "OK" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.GetPrintLastSettings") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$v</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$v </Calculation>
    <Field table="PDFKit Print" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$v </Calculation>
    <Field table="PDFKit Print" id="99999" name="Settings "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"OK" </Calculation>
    <Field table="PDFKit Print" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.GetPrintOption

parameters.name: Key

functions.name: PDFKit.GetPrintSetting
functions.description: Queries print settings for printing PDF. See also PDFKit.Print function. See also PDFKit.SetPrintSetting where options are described. You can query default printer name using "DefaultPrinter" as selector. New in 8.1 you can use selector LastSettings to query last settings as XML and discover what options are set by using the dialog, e.g. the tray names. Print settings for iOS to query:

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to query.

examples.label: Query printer:
examples.xml_code:

functions.name: PDFKit.NewPDFDocument
functions.description: Creates a new empty PDF and returns a PDF Reference value. This value can be used in other PDFKit calls to work on the same PDF. See also PDFKit.Open . PDF Document reference numbers are starting at 30000 and counting up for each new document.
functions.result: Returns a number for the PDF. Use PDFKit.Release later to free the memory.

examples.label: Create PDF from images:
examples.text_code:
# Start new document
Set Variable [
$doc
; Value:
MBS
("PDFKit.NewPDFDocument")]
# Add image in full size as new page
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.AddImagePage
";
$doc
;
test::image
)]
# Add empty page in default size
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.AddEmptyPage
";
$doc
)]
# Add image in given size as new page:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.AddImagePage
";
$doc
;
test::image
; 400; 300)]
# get PDF document and store in container:
Set Field [
test::test
;
MBS
("
PDFKit.GetPDFDocument
";
$doc
)]
# release memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$doc
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Start new document</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.NewPDFDocument")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$doc</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add image in full size as new page</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.AddImagePage"; $doc; test::image)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add empty page in default size</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.AddEmptyPage"; $doc)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add image in given size as new page:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.AddImagePage"; $doc; test::image; 400; 300)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get PDF document and store in container:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.GetPDFDocument"; $doc)</Calculation>
    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="test"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $doc)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.Open
functions.description: Opens a PDF and returns a PDF Reference value. This value can be used in other PDFKit calls to work on the same PDF. See also PDFKit.OpenContainer , PDFKit.OpenPath and PDFKit.OpenURL . PDF Document reference numbers are starting at 30000 and counting up for each new document. If you need to open a PDF file in the PDF Viewer, please use Files.LaunchFile with the path to the PDF file instead.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL.

functions.result: Returns a number for the PDF. Use PDFKit.Release later to free the memory.

examples.label: Open the PDF from the container value:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Open"; myTable::myMediaField)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.OpenContainer
functions.description: Opens a PDF from container value and returns a PDF Reference value. This value can be used in other PDFKit calls to work on the same PDF. See also PDFKit.OpenPath and PDFKit.OpenURL . PDF Document reference numbers are starting at 30000 and counting up for each new document. Use PDFKit.Release later to free the memory.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: A media value containing PDF data.

functions.result: Returns a number for the PDF.

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Rotate page and set title:
examples.text_code:
# open PDF from container
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value:
MBS
("PDFKit.OpenContainer";
combine pdf::result
)]
# Set title for PDF
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.SetPDFAttribute
";
$ref
; "Title";
combine pdf::title
)]
# Rotate first page by 90°
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.SetPDFPageRotation
";
$ref
; 0; 90)]
# save document to container
Set Field [
combine pdf::result
;
MBS
("
PDFKit.GetPDFDocument
";
$ref
; "result.pdf")]
# free memory
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$ref
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> open PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.OpenContainer"; combine pdf::result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ref</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set title for PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.SetPDFAttribute"; $ref; "Title"; combine pdf::title)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Rotate first page by 90°</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.SetPDFPageRotation"; $ref; 0; 90)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> save document to container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.GetPDFDocument"; $ref; "result.pdf")</Calculation>
    <Field table="combine pdf" id="99999" name="result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $ref)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.OpenPath
functions.description: Opens a PDF from a file path and returns a PDF Reference value. This value can be used in other PDFKit calls to work on the same PDF. See also PDFKit.OpenContainer and PDFKit.OpenURL . PDF Document reference numbers are starting at 30000 and counting up for each new document. Use PDFKit.Release later to free the memory.

parameters.name: Path

functions.result: Returns a number for the PDF.

functions.name: PDFKit.OpenURL
functions.description: Opens a PDF from an URL and returns a PDF Reference value. This value can be used in other PDFKit calls to work on the same PDF. See also PDFKit.OpenPath and PDFKit.OpenContainer . PDF Document reference numbers are starting at 30000 and counting up for each new document. Use PDFKit.Release later to free the memory. Seems to work for HTTP protocol, too.  The Apple frameworks transparently handle this out of the control of the plugin. With HTTPS after redirect from HTTP is also possible. For more control, please use CURL functions.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: A text with an URL.

functions.result: Returns a number for the PDF.

examples.label: Open the PDF with the given URL:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.OpenURL"; "http://www.somedomain.invalid/somepath/test.pdf")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.PageBounds
functions.description: Returns the bounds for the specified PDF display box. Note that only the media box is required for a PDF. If you request the bounds for the crop box, but the PDF does not include a crop box, the bounds for the media box are returned instead. If you request the bounds for other box types, and the PDF does not includes these types, the bounds for the crop box are returned instead. The coordinates for the box are in page space, so you might need to transform the points if the page has a rotation on it. Also, note that the bounds boundsForBox returns are intersected with the page's media box.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the page. From zero to PDFKit.GetPDFPageCount -1.

parameters.name: Box
parameters.description: Optionally, the PDF box to use. Can be Media Box = 0, Crop Box = 1, Bleed Box = 2, TrimBox = 3 or ArtBox = 4. If the box is not defined, we fall back to the media box. Default is 0.

parameters.name: mm
parameters.description: Optionally. If you pass 1, we convert the rectangle to millimeters. Else we return points where 72 points are one inch. Default is 0.

functions.result: Returns four values separated with new line containing x, y, width and height.

examples.label: Get media box of third page in a PDF in a container field:

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.Print
functions.description: Prints a PDF without dialogs. Optionally if you have a fax printer, you can also pass fax number to send a fax. Please use PDFKit.ResetPrintSettings and then call PDFKit.SetPrintSetting for all the settings you need. Please use DynaPDF.Print for Windows. Printing may fail on Server if no printer selected or printer is not turned on. Added printing for iOS to the plugin in v14.2. You can print either with dialog or without dialog. You can print PDF documents here, image containers or file paths to image and PDF files.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

examples.label: Print a PDF container:
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

MyTable::MyDocumentContainer
)

examples.label: Print what is in the container:
examples.text_code:
# prints PDF and Images on iOS
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("PDFKit.Print";
PDFKit Print::FileContainer
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Set Field [
PDFKit Print::Result
;
$r
$r
]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to print file" ;
$r
]
Else
Set Field [
PDFKit Print::Result
;
$r
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> prints PDF and Images on iOS</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Print"; PDFKit Print::FileContainer) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r $r </Calculation>
    <Field table="PDFKit Print" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to print file" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r $r </Calculation>
    <Field table="PDFKit Print" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "ShowsPrintPanel"; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "PrinterURL"; "ipp://Laserdrucker.local.:631/ipp/print" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "OutputType"; "Grayscale" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "JobTitle"; "Test" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "DuplexMode"; "LongEdge" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Print"; PDFKit Print::FileContainer) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r $r </Calculation>
    <Field table="PDFKit Print" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to open file" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r $r </Calculation>
    <Field table="PDFKit Print" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.Release
functions.description: Releases a PDF reference.

parameters.name: PDFReference
parameters.description: A number referencing a PDF you opened before.

functions.result: Returns OK on success. Do no longer use the reference number after this call as the object has been freed and accessing it again can crash your application.

examples.label: Release the PDF reference in variable ref
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $ref)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Release all PDFKit documents. Be aware that on a server this may free objects, which are used by other scripts running in parallel!

examples.label: Cleanup:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.ReleaseAll" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.ResetPrintSettings
functions.description: Resets print settings to default settings. Default is what is setup in the shared print settings for the application.

functions.name: PDFKit.RestorePrintSettings
functions.description: Restores print settings.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container value with printer settings.

functions.name: PDFKit.SavePrintSettings
functions.description: Saves current print settings. Print settings are wrapped in a container value. You can store them in a container and later restore them with PDFKit.RestorePrintSettings or PrintDialog.SetConfiguration function.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use in the container value.

functions.result: Returns container value with print settings or error.

functions.name: PDFKit.SelectPrinter
functions.description: Shows the printer picker. On success, we return 1 if the user picked a printer or 0 if not. If a printer was selected before, we pre-select it for the next time you show the dialog. You can set the printer to pre-select using PDFKit.SetPrintSetting function. See PDFKit.GetPrintSetting to query printerURL to identify it.

examples.label: Pick a printer:
examples.text_code:
If [ Length (
PDFKit Print::PrinterURL
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.SetPrintSetting
"; "PrinterURL";
PDFKit Print::PrinterURL
) ]
End If
# show the dialog
Set Variable [
$userDidSelect
; Value:
MBS
( "PDFKit.SelectPrinter" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to show printer selecto…" ;
$r
]
Else If [
$userDidSelect
]
# and check what we got
Set Variable [
$printerURL
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.GetPrintSetting
"; "PrinterURL" ) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ Length ( PDFKit Print::PrinterURL ) > 0 ]
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "PrinterURL"; PDFKit Print::PrinterURL ) ]
End If
# show the dialog
Set Variable [ $userDidSelect ; Value: MBS( "PDFKit.SelectPrinter" ) ]
If [ MBS("IsError") ]
	Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to show printer selecto…" ; $r ]
Else If [ $userDidSelect ]
	# and check what we got
	Set Variable [ $printerURL ; Value: MBS( "PDFKit.GetPrintSetting"; "PrinterURL" ) ]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.SetCurrentFilter
functions.description: Sets the current filter. You can use the Quartz Filter Manager panel to create a panel. This is for all PDF saving in MBS Plugin with PDFKit functions. Applied to PDF in PDFKit.GetPDFPagesPDF , PDFKit.GetPDFPagePDF , PDFKit.Combine , PDFKit.WriteToPath and other functions writing PDFs.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the filter. Use QuartzFilterManager.Filters to get list of filters. If Filter name is empty or missing, the filter is removed.

examples.label: Set a filter:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.SetCurrentFilter"; "Compress Pictures")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.SetIgnoreCombineErrors
functions.description: Sets whether Combine operation should report error if you did not pass PDF files or references. If you call PDFKit.Combine or PDFKit.CombineRef and you pass several container values, those functions normally stop if one of the parameters is not a PDF reference of any kind. If you set errors to be ignored, you can still combine other PDFs.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 or 0.

examples.label: Ignore errors on Combine
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.SetIgnoreCombineErrors"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.SetPDFAttribute
functions.description: Sets a PDF attribute. While you can pass here a container value or an URL to a PDF file, you should really pass a reference value. Because once you changed the PDF attribute, you want to save the PDF with a call to PDFKit.GetDocument. New keys in v5.4: OwnerPassword, UserPassword, EncryptionKeyLength, AllowsPrinting and AllowsCopying.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A PDF reference from PDFKit.Open or other functions.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: Which information to set. This can be one of the following: Title, Subject, Author, Creator or Keywords.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value for this key. Either a text for text value or a timestamp for a date.

functions.result: Returns "OK" on success or an error message.

examples.label: Set title to a PDF:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("PDFKit.SetPDFAttribute";
$ref
; "title"; "The new title for this PDF file." )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.SetPDFAttribute"; $ref; "title"; "The new title for this PDF file." )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Rotate page and set title:
examples.text_code:
# open PDF from container
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.OpenContainer
";
combine pdf::result
)]
# Set title for PDF
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("PDFKit.SetPDFAttribute";
$ref
; "Title";
combine pdf::title
)]
# Rotate first page by 90°
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.SetPDFPageRotation
";
$ref
; 0; 90)]
# save document to container
Set Field [
combine pdf::result
;
MBS
("
PDFKit.GetPDFDocument
";
$ref
; "result.pdf")]
# free memory
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$ref
)]
examples.xml_code:

    <Text> open PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.OpenContainer"; combine pdf::result)</Calculation>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.SetPDFPageRotation"; $ref; 0; 90)</Calculation>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>

functions.name: PDFKit.SetPDFPageDisplayAnnotation
functions.description: Sets whether annotations are drawn. Function to turn on or off the display of annotations when the page is drawn. Your changes are only done in memory, so please remember to write modified pdf back to disk, e.g. by using PDFKit.WriteToPath . Or use PDFKit.GetPDFDocument to get a PDF for storing in a container.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF reference number.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the page. From zero to PDFKit.GetPDFPageCount -1.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value for whether to display annotations. Can be 0 or 1.

examples.label: Render with or without image:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.OpenContainer
";
PDFKit Rendering::PDF
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "PDFKit.SetPDFPageDisplayAnnotation";
$pdf
; 0; 0 )]
Set Field [
PDFKit Rendering::Image1
;
MBS
( "
PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage
";
$pdf
; 0)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "PDFKit.SetPDFPageDisplayAnnotation";
$pdf
; 0; 1 )]
Set Field [
PDFKit Rendering::Image2
;
MBS
( "
PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage
";
$pdf
; 0)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$pdf
)]
examples.xml_code:

    </Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">

functions.name: PDFKit.SetPDFPageRotation
functions.description: Sets the rotation angle for the page in degrees. The rotation must be a positive or negative multiple of 90 (negative angles are converted to their positive equivalents; for example, -90 is changed to 270). Your changes are only done in memory, so please remember to write modified pdf back to disk, e.g. by using PDFKit.WriteToPath . Or use PDFKit.GetPDFDocument to get a PDF for storing in a container.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the page. From zero to PDFKit.GetPDFPageCount -1.

parameters.name: Rotation
parameters.description: The new rotation value.

examples.label: Rotate page and set title:
examples.text_code:
# open PDF from container
Set Variable [
$ref
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.OpenContainer
";
combine pdf::result
)]
# Set title for PDF
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.SetPDFAttribute
";
$ref
; "Title";
combine pdf::title
)]
# Rotate first page by 90°
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("PDFKit.SetPDFPageRotation";
$ref
; 0; 90)]
# save document to container
Set Field [
combine pdf::result
;
MBS
("
PDFKit.GetPDFDocument
";
$ref
; "result.pdf")]
# free memory
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$ref
)]
examples.xml_code:

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.OpenContainer"; combine pdf::result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ref</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set title for PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.SetPDFAttribute"; $ref; "Title"; combine pdf::title)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Rotate first page by 90°</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.SetPDFPageRotation"; $ref; 0; 90)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> save document to container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.GetPDFDocument"; $ref; "result.pdf")</Calculation>
    <Field table="combine pdf" id="99999" name="result"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> free memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $ref)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.SetPrintOption
functions.description: Sets custom print options. For regular options, please use PDFKit.SetPrintSetting .

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key to use.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to use. Depending on data type, the plugin will pass a number or a text.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type of data to add. Default is empty to use type of value passed. So text or number type would be picked automatically. Valid values for type are boolean, text, number, integer and text. Integer is for integer numbers while number is for floating numbers.

examples.label: Sets a custom option:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintOption"; "xxx"; 123 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Sets a custom boolean option:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintOption"; "xxx"; 1; "boolean" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.SetPrintSetting
functions.description: Sets print settings for printing PDF. See also PDFKit.Print function. All settings are optional and if not set will use default setting. Possible settings:

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to set.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value for the setting.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "FaxNumber"; "+123456" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Print page 2 to 10 and make 3 copies:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.ResetPrintSettings
" )
MBS
( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "FirstPage"; 2 )
MBS
( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "LastPage"; 10 )
MBS
( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "Copies"; 3 )
MBS
( "
PDFKit.Print
" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.ResetPrintSettings" )
MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "FirstPage"; 2 )
MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "LastPage"; 10 )
MBS( "PDFKit.SetPrintSetting"; "Copies"; 3 )
MBS( "PDFKit.Print" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.Watermark
functions.signature: MBS( "PDFKit.Watermark"; PDF; PageNumber; Image; Transparency; X; Y { ; W; H; Background; DeltaX; DeltaY } )
functions.description: Adds a watermark to a page. This modifies an in memory PDF Document which you can create by opening one using PDFKit.Open , PDFKit.OpenContainer , PDFKit.OpenPath or PDFKit.OpenURL . You can watermark several pages by calling this function in a loop. Don't forget to use PDFKit.Release to later release memory. You can do similar with DynaPDF with insert image command ( DynaPDF.InsertImage ) and alpha set ( DynaPDF.SetAlpha ). Your changes are only done in memory, so please remember to write modified pdf back to disk, e.g. by using PDFKit.WriteToPath . Or use PDFKit.GetPDFDocument to get a PDF for storing in a container.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: PageNumber
parameters.description: The page number of the page to modify.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The image to be used for watermark. Should be a PNG with mask for best results. Pass a container value.

parameters.name: Transparency
parameters.description: The transparency level. From 0.0 (opaque) to 1.0 (full transparency).

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: X coordinate. If zero, the plugin will center the image. If negative, this value is the distance from right side.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: Y coordinate. As with all PDF coordinates, the 0 coordinate is on the bottom of the page. If zero, the plugin will center the image. If negative, this value is the distance from top side.

parameters.name: W
parameters.description: Desired width. If value is zero or not provided, the plugin will use the image width.

parameters.name: H
parameters.description: Desired height. If value is zero or not provided, the plugin will use the image height.

parameters.name: Background
parameters.description: Whether to put image in foreground or background. Default is foreground. Can be 1 for background or 0 for foreground. Does not work if existing page clears background with background color!

parameters.name: DeltaX
parameters.description: Additional positioning offset to be added after the calculations for above are done.

parameters.name: DeltaY
parameters.description: Additional positioning offset to be added after the calculations for above are done.

examples.label: Watermark with an image over full page:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Watermark"; $pdf; 0; Watermark PDF Page::WatermarkImage; 0; 0; 0; 0; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.WriteToPath
functions.description: Writes the PDF to the given file path. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: A valid native file path.

examples.label: Load PDF, Write PDF and release it.
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.OpenPath"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.pdf")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$h</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.WriteToPath"; $h; "/Users/cs/Desktop/output.pdf")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $h)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.allowsCopying
functions.description: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the document allows copying of content to the Pasteboard. The ability to copy content from a PDF document is an attribute unrelated to whether the document is locked or unlocked. It depends on the PDF permissions set by the document's author. This method only determines the desired permissions setting in the PDF document; it is up to the application to enforce (or ignore) the permissions. This method always returns 1 if the document is not encrypted. Note that in many cases an encrypted document may still be readable by all users due to the standard empty string password. For more details about user and owner passwords, see the Adobe PDF specification.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

functions.result: Returns 1 if copying is allowed and 0 else.

examples.label: Check directly if a PDF in a container allows copying:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.allowsCopying"; Test::Document )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Shows some properties:
examples.text_code:
# Load PDF from container
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.OpenContainer
";
Popup Menu::logo
) ]
# Check some properties
Set Variable [
$allowsCopying
; Value:
MBS
( "PDFKit.allowsCopying";
$PDF
) ]
Set Variable [
$allowsPrinting
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.allowsPrinting
";
$PDF
) ]
Set Variable [
$isEncrypted
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.isEncrypted
";
$PDF
) ]
Set Variable [
$isLocked
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.isLocked
";
$PDF
) ]
# show in dialog
Show Custom Dialog [ "PDF Details" ; "allows copying: " & If(
$allowsCopying
; "yes"; "no") & ¶ &
"allows printing: " & If(
$allowsPrinting
; "yes"; "no") & ¶ &
"is encrypted: " & If(
$isEncrypted
; "yes"; "no") & ¶ &
"is locked: " & If(
$isLocked
; "yes"; "no")]
# release PDF
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
PDFKit.Release
";
$pdf
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load PDF from container</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.OpenContainer"; Popup Menu::logo ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Check some properties</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.allowsCopying"; $PDF ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$allowsCopying</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.allowsPrinting"; $PDF ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$allowsPrinting</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.isEncrypted"; $PDF ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$isEncrypted</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PDFKit.isLocked"; $PDF ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$isLocked</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> show in dialog</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "PDF Details" ; "allows copying: " & If($allowsCopying; "yes"; "no") & ¶ &"allows printing: " & If($allowsPrinting; "yes"; "no") & ¶ & "is encrypted: " & If($isEncrypted; "yes"; "no") & ¶ & "is locked: " & If($isLocked; "yes"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"no")</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> release PDF</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PDFKit.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PDFKit.allowsPrinting
functions.description: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the document allows printing. The ability to print a PDF document is an attribute unrelated to whether the document is locked or unlocked. It depends on the PDF permissions set by the document's author. This method only determines the desired permissions setting in the PDF document; it is up to the application to enforce (or ignore) the permissions. This method always returns 1 if the document is not encrypted. Note that in many cases an encrypted document may still be readable by all users due to the standard empty string password. For more details about user and owner passwords, see the Adobe PDF specification.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .

functions.result: Returns 1 if printing is allowed and 0 else.

functions.name: PDFKit.isEncrypted
functions.description: Returns a Boolean value specifying whether the document is encrypted. If encrypted, reading the document requires a password. Encrypted documents whose password is the empty string are unlocked automatically upon opening, because PDF Kit tries the empty string as a password if none is supplied.

parameters.description: A container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open .
