functions.name: Preview.Create
functions.signature: MBS( "Preview.Create"; WindowRef; X; Y; W; H { ; DX; DY; classID } )
functions.description: Creates a new preview area inside the window with the given rectangle. A way to display a PDF Viewer in FileMaker independent of an interactive container. Available on Windows 10 and newer. If you use a register on the window, be aware that the drop area actually is in front of the register. So you need to check in the script which register page is current and should get the dropped files. Use Preview.Release later to destroy the preview area. Preview reference numbers are starting at 92000 and counting up for each new preview area. See HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PreviewHandlers in the registry for available preview handlers for Windows beside PDF Viewer one.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The left coordinate in the window.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The top coordinate in the window.

parameters.name: W
parameters.description: The width of the preview area in the window.

parameters.name: H
parameters.description: The height of the preview area in the window.

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to top or bottom.

parameters.name: classID
parameters.description: The class UUID for the preview handler to use.

functions.name: Preview.CreateWithControl
functions.signature: MBS( "Preview.CreateWithControl"; WindowRef; ControlName { ; DX; DY; classID } )
functions.description: Creates a new preview on the given window covering the area of the given control. A way to display a PDF Viewer in FileMaker independent of an interactive container. Available on Windows 10 and newer. If you use a register (tab controls) on the window, be aware that the preview actually is in front of the register (tab controls). You may hide the preview if another register is visible. Use Preview.Release later to destroy the text view. Preview reference numbers are starting at 92000 and counting up for each new text view. When creating on a card window, please pass reference to parent window or just 0 for front window. On MacOS you may need to pass delta y value to adjust position. If you create a control on a new window, you may do window update command in your script first to have FileMaker actually build the window with controls and display it. A short script pause or using FM.RunScriptLater with 0.1 delay can help having all controls draw themselves. Then you can use this command to put our control on top. We use GetLayoutObjectAttribute() to query left, top, width and height and this gives empty value if control is not yet here. Creation fails to pick the right window in case the window is selected by name and the window name is not unique. In that case better work with window reference numbers or just pass 0 for current front window. On macOS, this uses QuickLook preview, so you can show other file types if needed. See HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PreviewHandlers in the registry for available preview handlers for Windows beside PDF Viewer one.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: ControlName
parameters.description: The name of the control on your FileMaker form. e.g. you can place a rectangle there to define the position.

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to top or bottom.

parameters.name: classID
parameters.description: The class UUID for the preview handler to use.

examples.label: Create with placeholder control:
examples.text_code:
# let FileMaker draw layout
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
#
# now add control
Set Variable [
$$preview
; Value:
MBS
( "Preview.CreateWithControl"; 0; "placeholder") ]
#
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to create preview contr…" ;
$$preview
]
Set Variable [
$$preview
; Value: "" ]
End If
#
# load content
If [ not IsEmpty (
PDF Viewer::PDF
Container ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Preview.LoadContainer
";
$$preview
;
PDF Viewer::PDF
Container ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load container" ;
$r
]
End If
Else If [ not IsEmpty (
PDF Viewer::PDF
Path ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Preview.LoadFile
";
$$preview
;
PDF Viewer::PDF
Path ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load file" ;
$r
]
End If
End If

functions.name: Preview.GetAutoResizingMask
functions.description: Queries auto resizing mask. This defines whether view changes its size based on the surrounding window.

parameters.name: PreviewRef
parameters.description: The reference ID for this preview control.

functions.name: Preview.GetAutostarts
functions.description: Queries whether to auto start preview. Whether to allow previews of movie files to start playback automatically when displayed.

functions.name: Preview.List
functions.description: Queries list of all currently preview areas. This helps debugging, especially finding scripts which do not release their objects properly.

  </Step>

functions.name: Preview.LoadContainer
functions.description: Loads a PDF document from data in container. Depending on what the preview handler can do, we either pass PDF directly in memory or use a temporary file, which later gets deleted when closing. For MBS Plugin 16.1 on macOS this got optimized to also handle other file types better. You can preview Keynote, Numbers or Pages files now from a container as well as many other types.

parameters.name: PreviewRef
parameters.description: The reference ID for this preview control.

parameters.name: ContainerValue
parameters.description: The container value (variable or field) to read the PDF from.

examples.label: Load preview:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Preview.LoadContainer";
$$preview
;
PDF Viewer::PDF
Container ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load container" ;
$r
]
End If

      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>

    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Preview.LoadFile
functions.description: Loads a PDF document from file path. If the file path is invalid, the preview will show a file not found error message. On macOS, this uses QuickLook preview, so you can show many common file types, including the following: iWork documents Microsoft Office documents Rich text format, or RTF, documents PDF files Images Text files with a uniform type identifier that conforms to the public.text type. To learn more, see Uniform type identifiers. Comma-separated values, or CSV, files 3D models in the USDZ format with both standalone and AR views for viewing the model The list of supported common file types may change between operating system releases. In addition, you can enable previews for your own file types by implementing your own preview extension. Showing Word files may require to install Pages, Excel files require to install Numbers and Powerpoint files require to install Keynote application. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: PreviewRef
parameters.description: The reference ID for this preview control.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native file path to the PDF file.

examples.label: Load file:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Preview.LoadFile";
$$preview
;
PDF Viewer::PDF
Path ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load file" ;
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Preview.LoadFile"; $$preview; PDF Viewer::PDF Path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to load file" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ProcessActivity.beginActivity
functions.description: Begins an activity. Pass in an activity to this function, and a non-empty reason string. Indicate completion of the activity by calling the corresponding ProcessActivity.endActivity function with the result of this function. The reason string is used for debugging. Requires macOS 10.9 and newer. This function allows to deactivate App Nap for FileMaker. So when a script has to do some work, you can disable system/display sleep here which also disables app nap. Added Windows support with version 13.0. Supported flags are IdleDisplaySleepDisabled, IdleSystemSleepDisabled, UserInitiated and UserInteractive. See also iOSApp.SetIdleTimerDisabled function.

parameters.name: activity
parameters.description: The activity as a number or a text. The plugin allows you to pass texts like IdleDisplaySleepDisabled, IdleSystemSleepDisabled, UserInitiated, UserInitiatedAllowingIdleSystemSleep, Background and LatencyCritical. Those texts can be combined.

parameters.name: Reason
parameters.description: The reason text. May appear in a log for macOS.

examples.label: Start an activity:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProcessActivity.beginActivity"; "IdleSystemSleepDisabled UserInitiated"; "Doing Backup in FileMaker" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Avoid display sleep:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ProcessActivity.endActivity
functions.description: Ends the activity started with ProcessActivity.beginActivity .

parameters.name: ProcessActivityID
parameters.description: The process activity reference number as returned by ProcessActivity.beginActivity function.

functions.name: StoreKit.AddPayment
functions.description: Adds a new payment to the transaction query. When a payment request is added to the queue, the payment queue processes that request with the Apple App Store and arranges for payment from the user. When that transaction is complete or if a failure occurs, the payment queue sends the payment transaction object that encapsulates the request to all transaction observers. Use StoreKit.Transactions later to query status. Clears last error state.

parameters.name: productIdentifier
parameters.description: The product identifier.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: The quantity of the product. Default is 1.

examples.label: Start order of item:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "StoreKit.AddPayment"; "123"; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: StoreKit.CanMakePayments
functions.description: Indicates whether the user is allowed to make payments. Returns 1 if the user is allowed to authorize payment. 0 if they do not have permission. An iPhone can be restricted from accessing the Apple App Store. For example, parents can restrict their children’s ability to purchase additional content. Your application should confirm that the user is allowed to authorize payments before adding a payment to the queue. Your application may also want to alter its behavior or appearance when the user is not allowed to authorize payments.

examples.label: Check status:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "StoreKit.CanMakePayments" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: StoreKit.FinishTransaction
functions.description: Completes a pending transaction. Your application should call this method on pending transaction to finish them. Calling StoreKit.FinishTransaction on a transaction removes it from the queue. Your application should call finishTransaction only after it has successfully processed the transaction and unlocked the functionality purchased by the user. Calling StoreKit.FinishTransaction on a transaction that is in the Purchasing state returns an error. Clears last error state.

parameters.name: TransactionID
parameters.description: The ID of the transaction. Or the zero based index, e.g. 0 for the first one.

functions.name: StoreKit.InvalidProductIdentifiers
functions.description: Queries invalid product identifiers. Please call StoreKit.QueryProducts first to query products and wait for StoreKit.ProductRequestRunning returning 0.

functions.name: StoreKit.IsProductViewVisible
functions.description: Whether the product view dialog is visible. This function is free to use. Created 22nd April 2018 , last changed 22nd April 2018 StoreKit.InvalidProductIdentifiers - StoreKit.LastError 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: StoreKit.LastError
functions.description: Queries last error as JSON. The error information includes code, domain and localized message. If no error happened, the error code is zero.

functions.name: StoreKit.ProductQueryRunning
functions.description: Whether a product query is running. Returns 1 if query started by StoreKit.QueryProducts is still running in background.

functions.name: StoreKit.Products
functions.description: Queries product list. Please call StoreKit.QueryProducts first to query products and wait for StoreKit.ProductRequestRunning returning 0. The JSON contains an array of products and each product has the following values: localizedDescription, localizedTitle, priceLocale, price, productIdentifier, downloadable and formattedPrice.

examples.label: Queries list of products:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "StoreKit.Products" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$products</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: StoreKit.QueryProducts
functions.description: Queries products. Your app uses an StoreKit.QueryProducts and StoreKit.Products to present localized prices and other information to the user without having to maintain that list of product information itself. Please call in start script of solution, so a few seconds later when you want to call StoreKit.Products the information is available. Clears last error state.

parameters.name: productIdentifiers
parameters.description: The list of product identifiers to check.

examples.label: Query for two products:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "StoreKit.QueryProducts"; "123¶456" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: StoreKit.RequestReview
functions.description: Shows review dialog. Tells StoreKit to ask the user to rate or review your app, if appropriate. Works asynchronously, so function returns immediately and starts dialog later. Although you should call this method when it makes sense in the user experience flow of your app, the actual display of a rating/review request view is governed by App Store policy. Because this method may or may not present an alert, it's not appropriate to call it in response to a button tap or other user action. Note When you call this method while your app is still in development mode, a rating/review request view is always displayed so that you can test the user interface and experience. However, this method has no effect when you call it in an app that you distribute using TestFlight. When you call this method in your shipping app and a rating/review request view is displayed, the system handles the entire process for you. In addition, you can continue to include a persistent link in the settings or configuration screens of your app that deep-links to your App Store product page. To automatically open a page on which users can write a review in the App Store, append the query parameter action=write-review to your product URL. Clears last error state.

functions.name: StoreKit.RestoreCompletedTransactions
functions.description: Asks the payment queue to restore previously completed purchases. Your application calls this method to restore transactions that were previously finished so that you can process them again. For example, your application would use this to allow a user to unlock previously purchased content onto a new device. When you create a new product to be sold in your store, you choose whether that product can be restored or not. See the In-App Purchase Programming Guide for more information. The payment queue will deliver a new transaction for each previously completed transaction that can be restored. Each transaction includes a copy of the original transaction. Clears last error state. Later when answer is received, we update last error.

parameters.name: UserName
parameters.description: An opaque identifier for the user’s account on your system.

functions.name: StoreKit.SetProductViewFinishedTrigger
functions.description: Sets trigger for product view. Script is triggered when the dialog showed via StoreKit.ShowProductView finished.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: Parameter to pass.

functions.name: StoreKit.SetRequestFailedTrigger
functions.description: Sets the script trigger for a failed request.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

functions.name: StoreKit.SetRestoreCompletedTransactionsTrigger
functions.description: Sets script triggered when transactions are restored. The parameter to the script is either OK or the error from restoring. Since version 15.0 the error number is used as prefix to the error message with a double colon.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

functions.name: StoreKit.SetUpdatedTransactionsTrigger
functions.description: Sets the script to trigger when transactions change. This method may be called often when something changes on the transactions. So you can check if they still match your state and process changes. This function is free to use. Created 22nd April 2018 , last changed 22nd April 2018 StoreKit.SetRestoreCompletedTransactionsTrigger - StoreKit.ShowProductView 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: Parameter to pass.

functions.name: StoreKit.ShowProductView
functions.description: Shows a new product screen to display. Be aware that this works asynchronously and the functions returns directly while the dialog shows later. If App Store doesn't return information, nothing happens. Clears last error state and later sets it depending on whether the viewer shows.

parameters.name: iTunesItemIdentifier
parameters.description: The ID of the product to show. Must be a number.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: StoreKit.Transactions
functions.description: Returns an array of pending transactions as JSON. For each transaction, you find values for transactionState, error, payment, originalTransaction, transactionDate and transactionIdentifier. State can be Purchasing, Purchased, Failed, Restored or Deferred.

    <Name>$transactions</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Translation.Available
functions.description: Queries whether translation is available. This checks if you have macOS 15 or iOS 18. Translation doesn't work server side as the background process for translation isn't available.

examples.label: Check for availability:

MBS
( "Translation.Available" ) = 1 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Translation is available" ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Translation is not available" ]
End If

End If</Calculation>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: Translation.CanRequestDownloads
functions.description: Queries whether this session can download. A boolean value that indicates whether a translation session can request language downloads. If this property is true, then the system prompts the person to authorize downloading additional languages if they aren't already installed. If this property is false, then the session cannot request language downloads and will throw the error TranslationError/notInstalled if you attempt to translate and the languages aren't installed. This is currently always 0 as the plugin doesn't allow GUI interaction. Requires macOS 26.0 or iOS 26.0 or later. This function is free to use. Created 24th November 2025 , last changed 16th December 2025 Translation.Available - Translation.DetectLanguage 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: SessionID
parameters.description: The translation session.

functions.name: Translation.DetectLanguage
functions.description: Detects the language of a given text. Returns the language identifier detected. Uses Apple's frameworks which use probabilities.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to analyze. Needs be long enough to make it correct. One or two words may not be enough!

examples.label: Try it for an English text:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Translation.IsReady
functions.description: Checks whether the session is ready to translate. Queries a boolean value that indicates whether the system has installed the source and target languages of the session and is ready to begin translation. Use this property to check whether a translation is likely to succeed. In a session that can request downloads, it prompts the person to approve downloads if languages aren't ready yet. In a session that can't request downloads, it will throw an error if the person calls any functions when languages aren't ready. Note: This value returns whether the languages are currently installed. The person or system can still delete the downloaded languages after calling this, so make sure you handle the appropriate errors when calling one of the translate functions. Requires macOS 15/26 or iOS 18/26 or later.

parameters.name: SessionID
parameters.description: The translation session.

examples.label: Check status:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$session
; Value:
MBS
( "
Translation.StartSession
"; "de"; "en") ]
If [
MBS
( "Translation.IsReady";
$session
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "We can translate German to English." ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Please go to system preferences. You may need to download language files." ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Translation.Release
";
$session
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Translation.StartSession"; "de"; "en") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$session</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS( "Translation.IsReady"; $session ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "We can translate German to English." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Please go to system preferences. You may need to download language files." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Translation.Release"; $session) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Translation.List
functions.description: Lists all identifiers for translation sessions. This function is free to use. Created 24th November 2025 , last changed 24th November 2025 Translation.IsReady - Translation.PrepareTranslation 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: Translation.PrepareTranslation
functions.description: Prepares translation. Asks for permission to download translation languages without doing any translations. If you know ahead of time which languages the person needs to translate, you can prompt them to download those languages in advance by calling this method. When you call this method, the framework asks the person for permission to download the sourceLanguage and targetLanguage. If the languages are already installed or in the middle of downloading, the function returnswithout prompting them. If you call this function when the sourceLanguage is empty, it unableToIdentifyLanguage error, since there's no sample text to identify which source language to use for translation. This function is free to use. Created 24th November 2025 , last changed 24th November 2025 Translation.List - Translation.Release 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: SessionID
parameters.description: The translation session.

functions.name: Translation.Release
functions.description: Frees the translation session.

parameters.name: SessionID
parameters.description: The translation session.

functions.name: Translation.ReleaseAll

functions.name: Translation.SourceLanguage
functions.description: Queries the input language to translate from. If this value is set to empty, the session tries to identify the source language automatically. If it can't identify the language, it prompts the person to choose the source language to use for the translation. Note: This value doesn't update after translation. To see what source language the session uses for a particular translation, check the response sourceLanguage property.

parameters.name: SessionID

functions.name: Translation.StartSession
functions.description: Begins a new session. Creates a translation session to translate between a given source and target language already installed on device. If one or both of the languages aren't installed on the device already, attempting to translate will throw errors.

parameters.name: fromLanguageIdentifier
parameters.description: The language to translate from.

parameters.name: toLanguageIdentifier
parameters.description: The language to translate from. If empty will default to the users current locale.

examples.label: Translate a text:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$session
; Value:
MBS
( "Translation.StartSession"; "de"; "en") ]
# translate something
Set Variable [
$translation
; Value:
MBS
( "
Translation.TranslateString
";
$session
; "Hallo Leute" ) ]
# show to user
Show Custom Dialog [
$translation
]
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Translation.Release
";
$session
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Translation.StartSession"; "de"; "en") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$session</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> translate something</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Translation.TranslateString"; $session; "Hallo Leute" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$translation</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> show to user</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> $translation </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Translation.Release"; $session) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Translation.Status
functions.description: Checks whether translation is available. Checks for the installation of a specific language pairing and whether it's ready for translation. Use this function to check whether the system has installed the required language assets and the languages are ready to use for translation. Note: The framework doesn't support translating from and to the same language. For example, you can't translate from English (US) to English (UK). FromLanguageIdentifier: The source language of the content. ToLanguageIdentifier: The target language you want to translate content into. When set to empty text, the system picks an appropriate target based on the person's preferred languages and returns the status for those languages. Provides the availability status for a language pairing via the delegate later. Installed languages models are stored in the mobile assets folder: /System/Library/AssetsV2/com_apple_MobileAsset_SpeechTranslationAssets7

parameters.name: fromLanguageIdentifier
parameters.description: The language to translate from.

parameters.name: toLanguageIdentifier
parameters.description: The language to translate from. If empty will default to the users current locale.

functions.result: Returns installed, supported, unsupported, unknown or an error.

examples.label: Can translate from German to English?
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Translation.Status"; "en"; "de" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Can translate from English to English?
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Translation.TranslateString
functions.description: Translates a single string of text. This function translates a single line of text and might display different UI depending on the state of the translation. The required languages for translation don't have to be installed before calling this method. If the required languages for translation have already downloaded and the source language is clear, this function returns results without showing any UI to the person. If the source or target language aren't installed, the framework asks the person for permission to download the languages. During the download a progress indicator displays. After it completes, the framework performs the translation. If the sourceLanguage is empty and the framework can't detect the source language from the content, the framework prompts the person to choose the source language. This function throws an error if: The person doesn't agree to downloading the languages The person dismisses the progress view during language downloads TranslationSession fails system validation The session doesn't allow requesting downloads and languages aren't installed You already cancelled the session Something goes wrong during translation If a person dismisses the progress view while the languages download, the system throws a user cancelled error, and the languages continue to download in the background. Note: This function call can take several minutes while languages download.

parameters.name: SessionID
parameters.description: The translation session.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to translate.

examples.label: Translate text:
examples.text_code:
Let([
session =
MBS
( "
Translation.StartSession
"; "de"; "en");
text =
MBS
( "Translation.TranslateString"; session; "Hallo Leute");
r =
MBS
("
Translation.Release
"; session)
]; text)
Example result:
Hallo Leute
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  session = MBS( "Translation.StartSession"; "de"; "en");
  text = MBS( "Translation.TranslateString"; session; "Hallo Leute");
  r = MBS("Translation.Release"; session)
]; text)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Translation.Translations
functions.description: Translates several texts. Translates multiple strings of text of the same language, returning the results all at once when complete. This function translates multiple strings as a batch and might display different UI depending on the state of the translation. The languages the framework requires for the translation don't have to be installed before calling this method. Pass in the strings of text you want to translate as an array of the Request type. This method takes longer to return than translateBatch, but it has the advantage of not having to map translation requests to responses. The responses return in the same order the requests are sent. If the required languages for translation have already downloaded and the source language is clear, this function returns results without showing any further prompts to the person. If the source or target language aren't installed, the framework asks the person for permission to download the languages. During download a progress indicator displays. After the download completes, the framework performs the translation. If the sourceLanguage is empty and the framework can't detect the source language from the content, the framework prompts the person to choose the source language. The framework only supports string translations of the same language. The strings must either match the sourceLanguage you set in the configuration, or if the sourceLanguage is empty, be of the same language. This function throws an error if: The person doesn't agree to downloading the languages The person dismisses the progress view during language downloads TranslationSession fails system validation The session doesn't allow requesting downloads and languages aren't installed You already cancelled the session Something goes wrong during translation If a person dismisses the progress view while the languages download, the system throws a user cancelled error, and the languages continue to download in the background. Note: Calls to this function can take several minutes while languages download.

parameters.name: SessionID
parameters.description: The translation session.

parameters.name: Requests
parameters.description: Pass a JSON array with entries for each request. Each request must have a sourceText value. Optional it can contain a clientIdentifier value, that is copied into the response.

examples.label: Try translations:
examples.text_code:
Let([
session =
MBS
( "
Translation.StartSession
"; "de"; "en");
json = "[]";
json = JSONSetElement ( json ; "[+].sourceText" ; "Hallo Leute" ; JSONString );
json = JSONSetElement ( json ; "[+].sourceText" ; "Man sieht sich bei der Konferenz!" ; JSONString );
text =
MBS
( "Translation.Translations"; session; json);
r =
MBS
("
Translation.Release
"; session)
]; text)
Example result:

  {
"targetText"
:
"Hello people"
,
"sourceLanguage"
:
"de"
,
"targetLanguage"
:
"en"
,
"sourceText"
:
"Hallo Leute"
},
  {
"sourceText"
:
"Man sieht sich bei der Konferenz!"
,
"sourceLanguage"
:
"de"
,
"targetText"
:
"See you at the conference!"
,
"targetLanguage"
:
"en"
}
]

  session = MBS( "Translation.StartSession"; "de"; "en");
  json = "[]";
  json = JSONSetElement ( json ; "[+].sourceText" ; "Hallo Leute" ; JSONString );
  json = JSONSetElement ( json ; "[+].sourceText" ; "Man sieht sich bei der Konferenz!" ; JSONString );

functions.name: WindowsLocation.Location
functions.description: Queries current location report. We return a JSON object which may contain the following values:

parameters.name: Longitude
parameters.description: The longitude, in degrees. The longitude is between -180 and 180, where East is positive.

parameters.name: Altitude
parameters.description: The altitude, in meters. The Altitude property retrieves the altitude relative to the reference ellipsoid that is defined by the latest revision of the World Geodetic System (WGS 84), rather than the altitude relative to sea level.

parameters.name: ErrorRadius
parameters.description: Combined with the location reported as the origin, this radius describes the circle in which the actual location is probably located.

parameters.name: AltitudeError
parameters.description: The altitude error, in meters.

parameters.name: SensorID
parameters.description: The ID of the sensor that generated the location report. Provided as formatted UUID.

parameters.name: Timestamp
parameters.description: The date and time when the report was generated. Time stamps are provided as Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).

examples.label: Query location:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$location
;
MBS
( "WindowsLocation.Location" ) ]
Example result:
{
"Latitude"
:
50.3975
,
"Longitude"
:
7.362
,
"Altitude"
:
0
,
"ErrorRadius"
:
7919
,
"AltitudeError"
:
0
,
"SensorID"
:
"{00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000}"
,
"Timestamp"
:
"7/30/2020 1:47:15 PM"
}
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WindowsLocation.Location" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$location</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
